sessions.title: Bianca Scout and Elena Isolini (Daughter Mary) Dance Workshop
sessions.details: Workshop developing “Act 1” of Daughter Mary / Anon’s Ballet: classical ballet with unrestricted gestures, scene making and character study. 35 Britannia Row, London. Part of Mica Georgis’s residency at The Writers’ Room, Florence Trust. Open to dancers and non-dancers. N.B. venue not accessible (studio up two flights of stairs). Tickets on Gel.

sessions.title: Zahra Van Nguyen Dance Workshop
sessions.details: Somatics and improvisational workshop: movement as narrative, lyricism and dramatic structures, text and score. 35 Britannia Row, London. Part of Mica Georgis’s residency at The Writers’ Room, Florence Trust. Open to dancers and non-dancers. N.B. venue not accessible (studio up two flights of stairs). Tickets on Gel.

sessions.title: Mya Cavner x Ballet for Non-Dancers Dance Workshop
sessions.details: Ballet for Non-Dancers: intentional movement, sensuality and presence. 35 Britannia Row, London. Part of Mica Georgis’s residency at The Writers’ Room, Florence Trust. Open to dancers and non-dancers. N.B. venue not accessible (studio up two flights of stairs). Tickets on Gel.

sessions.title: Jules Cunningham Dance Workshop
sessions.details: Workshop with Jules Cunningham: form, gender identity and collaborative movement language. 35 Britannia Row, London. Part of Mica Georgis’s residency at The Writers’ Room, Florence Trust. Open to dancers and non-dancers.

sessions.title: All Salsa Festival
sessions.details: Markadas Dance Center, 20 Av du Dr Schweitzer, Lyon, 69330, France • Accueil https://www.instagram.com/allsalsafestival CUBAN MAMBO CALEÑA All Salsa Festival JUNE 24-28 LYON – FRANCE More than 60 workshops, from Friday evening to Sunday noon, covering the full diversity of Salsa : Cuban – Mambo/Porto (On 1/On2) – Caleña, and their influences or derivatives (Bogaloo, Chachacha, Pachanga, Choke, Rumba, Orichas, Son, Palo…). 3 fully-equipped dance halls (parquet floors, mirrors, air-conditioning, large volumes, podiums, video broadcasting) + 1 outdoor space for Sun & Fun classes. An intense program based on individual workshops (Footworks, Musicality, Spins, Body Mouv, Choregraphic Expression, Styling…), not forgetting, of course, workshops for partners. ATTENTION – Be reasonable and don’t set yourself the goal of doing everything, even with your Full Pas – our program is too rich! (think poses, water, dried fruit…) Level workshops (classified yellow = Min. 8 months of practice / Orange = Min. 1 year / Red = Min. 2 years), and workshops accessible to all, whatever your level or style of salsa (code green), so that everyone can really work and progress at their own pace and according to their own needs (so please respect the levels, and don’t think you’ll learn more with a workshop above your level, on the contrary!) Accessible artists for all levels Even the likes of Wilmer y Maria, Bersy Cortez, Seo Fernandez, Tania Cannarsa, Marco Ferrigno, Yusimi Moya or Husory… Will take part in classes at different levels, so that everyone can benefit from their unique talents! Also, Master & Specific Classes, not included in a Full Pass, will allow you to push your level up (only for higgh level dancers). You’ll be able to move from room to room, workshop to workshop, style to style (no need to book in advance)… To take advantage of all the richness of All Salsa, or to concentrate on your Salsa Passion (although we strongly recommend you open up your styles and desires!). Our priority – to help you progress through diversity, while having fun !

sessions.title: Cubaneando
sessions.details: La Pollera Colora, La Pollera Colora, 4-6 London Bridge St,, London, SE1 9SG, United Kingdom • Payment for the sessions will be collected by cash at the door, However, you can still pay by card for your drinks at the Bar and the cloakroom. Classes and Social £10 for 1h or £15 if you do 2h – A Nightclub “Standing” entrance is included with the lessons. (Club only entry fee is £10) If you would like to reserve a table, please contact the venue directly on: 0730 864 9434 Cuban Salsa 7 & 8:15pm (2 levels each) with Esther Alvero & Antonio de Freitas1

sessions.title: MiCUBA! Matinées — Damarys' Birthday Special
sessions.details: 90 Brixton Road, Oval, 90 Brixton Road, Oval, London, SW9 6BE, United Kingdom • 🇨🇺 MiCUBA! Matinées — Damarys Birthday Celebration A Journey Through the Heart of Cuba 📅 Saturday 27th June 2026 🕒 3pm – 9pm 📍 Oval, South London Hosted & Curated by Damarys Farres ✨ A Special Birthday Edition of MiCUBA! Join us for a unique Cuban cultural celebration as MiCUBA! marks Damarys’ birthday with an afternoon and evening of dance, live music, percussion, culture, food, community and joy. Rumba meets Son in a vibrant journey through Cuban traditions where music, movement and connection come together in true MiCUBA! style. Expect live music, workshops, social dancing, cultural experiences and a warm Cuban family atmosphere. Immersive, inspiring and 110% authentic. 🎉 Everyone is Welcome Whether you are a complete beginner, experienced dancer, musician, cultural explorer, family member or simply curious about Cuba, MiCUBA! welcomes you. No partner required. No previous experience necessary. This is a safe, inclusive and welcoming space where people come together to learn, connect and celebrate Cuban culture. MiCUBA! is about connection, not levels. 🇨🇺 The MiCUBA! Experience • Cuban dance & musicality workshops • Live percussion workshop • Live Cuban music • Cultural insights and demonstrations • Social dancing • Latin food & drinks available • Birthday celebration surprises • Community vibes and authentic Cuban energy ✨ From learning to dancing, from listening to celebrating — this is Cuba in London. Immersive, inspiring and 110% authentic. All welcome 💃 Workshops & Programme 3pm – 6pm Rumba Meets Son Explore the elegance, rhythm and cultural roots of Son Cubano alongside the grounding energy and expression of Cuban Rumba. Including: • Partner connection • Lead & follow dynamics • Rhythm & musicality • Social dance application • Cuban culture & history Suitable for all levels. 🪘 Percussion Workshop With Randy Lester (Santiago de Cuba) • Traditional Cuban rhythms • Understanding drum, clave and dance • Practical hands-on learning • Open to all levels 🌙 Social & Birthday Celebration 6pm – 9pm • Live music performances • Social dancing • Cuban vibes & DJ sets • Food & drinks available • Birthday celebration atmosphere 🎶 Featured Artists Damarys Farres Cuban-born dancer, choreographer and founder of DCubanSchool of Arts, dedicated to sharing authentic Cuban and Afro-Cuban culture internationally. Homero González Cuban teacher, performer bringing authentic Cuban energy and tradition to the MiCUBA! experience. Anier Jova Cuban teacher known for sharing the richness, passion and spirit of Cuban Salsa and Timba. Randy Lester Percussionist from Santiago de Cuba bringing powerful Afro-Caribbean rhythms and deep musical heritage. Together they bring the spirit, sound, and soul of Cuba to life. 🎭 Dress Code & Party Spirit Inspired by Cuban elegance and tropical celebration: ❤️ Red 💙 Blue 🤍 White 🌴 Tropical colours Feel free to bring: • Party masks • Hats • Flowers • Fans • Balloons • Your best Cuban celebration energy Workshops: Comfortable clothing and suitable dance shoes. Matinee Celebration: Smart casual or elegant Cuban style. 📍 Location 90 Brixton Road, Oval London SW9 6BE • Oval Station – 5 minutes walk • Parking nearby • Excellent transport links 🎟 Tickets • Single Workshop • Workshop Pass • Percussion Only • Social & Celebration Pass • Full Experience (Recommended) • Food Packages Advance booking strongly recommended. Limited capacity. 🎉 Celebrating Something Special? Birthday? Anniversary? Group outing? MiCUBA! Matinées is the perfect place to celebrate in a vibrant, joyful Cuban atmosphere. For group bookings, birthday packages, NHS or NUS discounts, please contact us directly for availability and details. By purchasing a ticket and attending the event, you agree to MiCUBA!’s Terms & Conditions. 🌞 One Day. One Celebration. One Cuban Family. Come dance, learn, connect and celebrate the heart of Cuba with us. Let’s dance. Let’s celebrate. 🇨🇺 ———————— 🌿 About MiCUBA! Once a month, South London transforms into Cuba. MiCUBA! is an authentic Cuban cultural experience, curated by Damarys Farres, bringing together live music, dance, percussion, food, culture, education and community in one vibrant celebration. Open to dancers and non-dancers of all levels, it is a welcoming space to connect, learn and experience the rhythm, dance, stories and spirit of Cuba — right here in London. 🇨🇺 🌺 Who Can Attend? Everyone is welcome: ✔ Beginners ✔ Experienced dancers ✔ Non-dancers ✔ Music lovers ✔ Cultural explorers ✔ Families & friends ✔ Dance teachers and promoters No partner required. No experience needed. MiCUBA! is about connection, not levels. Important Reminder: Let’s DANCE!

sessions.title: Casino Libre
sessions.details: Farr’s Dalston, 17-19 Dalston Ln,, London, E8 3DF, United Kingdom • Casino (Cuban Salsa) Partnerwork Classes Mondays | 7:00–11:00 PM Drop-in classes | 3 Levels Join us for a focused evening of Casino (Cuban Salsa) partnerwork, dedicated to building strong foundations, clean technique, and confident social dancing all rooted in authentic Cuban style. These drop-in classes are designed to support dancers at different stages, with three progressive levels running back-to-back so you can join exactly where you feel comfortable. Class Structure Intro: For beginners and those new to Casino. Learn the essential basics of Cuban partnerwork, connection, flow, and movement in a friendly, supportive environment. Improvers: For dancers with some experience. Strengthen your foundations, refine technique, and develop better flow, timing, and partner communication. Basics. For confident dancers ready to deepen their skills. Explore more complex partnerwork, musical interpretation, variation, and technique with a strong focus on clarity, control, and social usability. What to Expect Clear, structured teaching Strong emphasis on correct Casino technique Partner rotation (no need to bring a partner change partners) A relaxed but focused atmosphere Practical material you can use immediately on the social dance floor Our approach prioritises quality over quantity, helping you build a solid technical base while staying true to the social, playful spirit of Cuban dance. Whether you’re just starting out or looking to sharpen your skills, these classes offer a consistent space to learn, practice, and grow.

sessions.title: Rueda Libre Wednesday
sessions.details: Coal Drops Yard, Coal Drops Yard, King's Cross, London, N1C 4DQ, United Kingdom • Join Us for an Unforgettable Outdoor Cuban Salsa Event! Get ready to dance, learn, meet amazing people, and embrace the joy of salsa dancing! Our outdoor salsa event is the perfect place to spice up your evening, whether you’re a seasoned pro or have never danced a step in your life. Here’s what to expect: Cuban Salsa and Rueda Classes: We’re offering classes for all levels, from absolute beginners to advanced dancers. No partner? No problem! Everyone is welcome to join in the fun. Visit https://ruedalibre.co.uk/classes/ to find out which class level suits you best. Class Time: Classes kick off at 7:00 PM, but make sure to arrive by 6:45 PM to show your QR code and collect your wristband. This ensures you’re ready and wont miss the beginning of the class. Free Outdoor Social Dancing: After the class, the night doesn’t end! Stick around for some free outdoor social dancing. Practice your moves, connect with the community, and just let loose. The vibe is 10/10 so enjoy it. Affordable Pricing: Classes start at just £9 (excluding fees), and you’ll have the chance to practice what you’ve learned during the session. The most budget-friendly way to pay is via our QR code in person, no fees to worry about. Prefer other options? We also accept cash, contactless payments, and online payments for your convenience. Whether you’re a salsa enthusiast or simply looking for a new experience, this is your chance to dance, laugh, and create unforgettable memories. Join us and make the most of your Wednesday evening. Ready to dance? Grab your spot and let’s make this a night to remember. See you there!

sessions.title: Casino Intensive with Ramses and Sandra
sessions.details: Saw Swee Hock Centre, Saw Swee Hock Centre, 1 Sheffield Street, London, WC2A 2AP, United Kingdom • Learn Cuban salsa from one of the best in the world After an incredible Casino bootcamp at La Dosis, the Cuban salsa festival in London, Ramses and Sandra are back to give two intensive classes on Casino Casino Fundamentals Here Ramses and Sandra will concentrate on walking patterns, turn patterns, frame and intention of the dance. how to move across the dance floor and look good doing it. Casino Elements In this class, Ramses and Sandra will elevate your casino by showing new turn patterns and techniques, and challenging you to use them in your dance. Expect corrections, new combinations and an eye opening experience. Casino Intensive is the above two classes at a cheaper rate. Please note: Fundamentals is not beginners. They won’t go over basic steps or beginner things such as timing, Dile que no and guapea. They will go over good techniques, walking patterns, turn patterns, positioning and frame. All fundamental things that people overlook and rush past. These are the things that make the difference in people’s dancing. Elements builds upon that and introduces new teen patterns in more complicated places and combinations that are challenging. Casino is very dynamic and you need to be aware of concepts and elements to improve your dancing

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Franziska Boehm
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Franzi, a German movement artist, performative researcher and teacher in London, takes great joy in integrating her musical background into her movement work. "I sing a sound which could become a melody, a process of sensing, or a movement". Franzi's session will draw on Skinner Releasing Technique (SRT) - using poetic imagery, supportive music and guided touch - to engage in movement and sound improvisation.

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Angela Andrew
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Angela is a London-based dance artist, teacher and producer who specializes in Lindy Hop and vernacular jazz. She's known for her unique teaching style and her passion for researching and spreading the roots of lindy hop. Lindy Hop is an African-American partner dance originating in the late 1920s Jazz Era in Harlem and across the USA. This fun and informative class will introduce the basics of Lindy Hop in a laid back, sociable setting - no partner needed and suitable for beginners.

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Amy Mauvan
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Amy is a New Zealand dancer and touring artist with a melting pot background of dance styles including modern and contemporary, bodyweather, dance theatre and live art. Amy is known for her creativity and accessibility working with imagery and stimuli. In this session we will use visualisation, movement phrases and improvisation to explore questions we might ask our younger and older selves.

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Galit's session shifted to next week
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • See below: we have cancelled Wednesday Moving on this day due to the heat. Please come next week to enjoy Galit's Reset and Juan's sound bath.

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Galit Criden with guest Juan Sanchez
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Galit's RESET movement method blends elements of somatic practices, contemporary dance, disco, and free improvisation. It's designed to take you on a transformative journey where you tap into your own power, explore movement and experience a sense of liberation. In this special extended session, Juan Sanchez will offer a sound bath to bring us into deep relaxation.

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Julia Testas
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Julia's improvisation sessions are inspired by a combination of her own somatic research, 'dissolving', and David Zambrano's concept of 'passing through', a philosophy that suggests that movement neeeds no leader in creation. Connecting, sharing and collaborating, Julia invites you to tap into your body's intelligence and cultivate greater confidence and freedom in your movement. The session encourages playful experimentation as you engage with others in a supportive environment.

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Heni Hale
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Heni is an independent artist, working as a choreographer, performer, researcher and educator in diverse contexts with a collaborative focus. We will work with landscapes, the interior moving landscapes of our bodies, the boney contours, structural architectures, and the soft fleshy fluid channels. We will explore different ways of seeing and being seen as we move, opening up a permissive, nourishing space. Exercises may involve working in pairs through some touch exercises, although there will always be alternatives.

sessions.title: Contemporary Dance StreamMovement Workshop
sessions.details: StreamMovement Dance Workshop ExperienceThis experience is all about connecting your body and mind with the body and mind of others in a kind and creative setting. Come connect with us and flow freely.In this in-person workshop we dive into the magic of StreamMovement dance techniques. Whether you're new or experienced, all are welcome to our studio where we combine improvisation, choreography, partnering and creation tasks along with physical training, breath awareness and somatic connection.

sessions.title: TRAPLORD: RELEASE THE TRAP - A Creative Wellbeing Workshop for Black Men
sessions.details: This workshop provides an intimate space for Black men to move, release, and be witnessed.To mark a decade of TRAPLORD, Ivan Michael Blackstock invites participants into a workshop that blends dance, street culture and conversation, offering a rare chance to explore vulnerability, identity, and emotional wellbeing through embodied practice and shared experience. Rooted in the creative world of the Olivier Award-winning production TRAPLORD.What to ExpectBreath work & Intention SettingArrive, ground, and call in your purpose for the session.Mind–Body Movement Session + CypherA guided movement practice followed by an open freestyle circle for expression, release, and shared energy.Q&A with Ivan Michael Blackstock + TRAPLORD FamilyA candid space for dialogue, reflection, and creative exchange.Exclusive Behind-the-Scenes Screening of TRAPLORDA private look at the creative process, unseen footage, and the evolution of the TRAPLORD world.A Gift of Flowers for the MandemBecause we believe in giving each other our flowers while we’re here — in body, spirit, and community.Who This Is For:This space is created specifically for Black men seeking a moment to reconnect with their bodies, their breath, and each other. Whether you are a dancer, a rapper, a creative or simply someone in need of release.No prior dance experience is required. Just come as you are.

sessions.title: Beginners Level 1 Ballet
sessions.details: For adults with little or no experience in Ballet, or for those who stopped ballet in their early teens. Each class builds form the last for true progress and there are handouts for each lesson with supporting videos. • Place: New City Fitness, Redvers St, London, N1 6NE • Dates: 17th September - 3rd December 2026(11 lessons; no class 29 Oct) • Cost: £170 • Teacher: Serena Cundari

sessions.title: Beginners Level 2 Ballet
sessions.details: For those who have completed Level 1 and are competent with its content including balance's (non-turning waltzes), chaine's (travelling turns on 2 feet) and some basic jumps; you will be able to confidently demonstrate the basics and have a good understanding of correct posture, placement, use of turnout and weight distribution. Also suitable for those who did lots of ballet until their mid to late teens and need to start back a little more slowly. There are handouts and supporting videos for each lesson. • Place: New City Fitness (Studio A), Redvers St, London, N1 6NE • Dates: 15th September - 1st December 2026(11 lessons; no class 27 Oct) • Cost: £170 • Teacher: Louise Blair

sessions.title: Beginners Level 3 Ballet (Monday)
sessions.details: For those who have competently completed Level 2 or for those at a similar stage who are confident with pas de valse en tournant (turning waltzes) and pique's turns (travelling turns on 1 leg) and able to perform continuous rises and releve's on one leg retaining placement and stability without the barre. This level introduces pirouettes and linking steps such as pas de bourree's and glissades and more allegro such as assemble', pas de chat and jete' ordinaire with faster allegro combinations. There are handouts and supporting videos for each lesson. • Place: New City Fitness (Studio A), Redvers Street, London, N1 6NE • Dates: 14th September - 30th November 2026(11 lessons; no class 26 Oct) • Cost: £170 • Teacher: Serena Cundari

sessions.title: Pre-Improvers
sessions.details:
This class focuses on building technical strength and confidence while polishing arm, head & leg coordination in preparation for Improvers 1. The Monday studio class will start with 10-15 mins of foot mobilising and strengthening exercises and body conditioning, particluarly good for those en pointe or training towards it. Suitable for those who are confident and polished with all Beginners 3 material (including Beginner-General) or for those already attending Improvers 1 who need a little extra help. 
There are supporting handouts and videos for each lesson.A 30 min add-on Pointe class for Complete Beginners follows the 90 minute class. • Place: New City Fitness (Studio A), Redvers Street, London, N1 6NE • Dates: 14th September - 30th November 2026(11 lessons; no class 26 Oct) • Cost: £190 / £255 • Teacher: Serena Cundari

sessions.title: Improvers 1
sessions.details:
Improvers 1 is for adults who are confident and polished with all the Beginners material up to and including Pre-Improvers and are ready to build on pirouettes, work on more challenging adage in the centre and start batterie. Some will benefit from attending Pre-Improvers alongside or instead, if not quite strong or polished enough yet.  There are supporting handouts and videos for each lesson. 

 You should already have attended the Complete Beginners Pointe course after Pre-Improvers on Mondays and/or be confident with relevé in retiré sideways at the barre and echappé sin the centre, before booking the Beginners Pointe class that follows. Those assessed as ready for Pointework should attend the Complete Beginners Pointe add-on with Pre-Improvers on Mondays first. • Place: Capital City College (Holloway), 444 Camden Road, London, N7 0SP • Dates: 16th September - 2nd December 2026(11 lessons; no class 28 Oct) • Cost: £185 / £250 • Teacher: Serena Cundari

sessions.title: Improvers 2
sessions.details: This class is for adults who have successfully mastered all the elements in Improvers 1 and are competent with double pirouettes and ready to learn fouettés, more complex batterie such as brisé and entrechat trois and more challenging grand allegro such as grand jeté en tournant, grand fouetté sauté and saut de basque. You should attend at least twice a week to be strong enough for this level; can also be taken with Improvers 1 if less advanced or with Advanced 1 if more experienced. Those attending pointework afterwards must already be sufficiently skilled and strong enough for relevés on one leg in the centre. This course is also suitable for ballet-fit returning dancers who received vocational training to a high level. A special discount package is available on Tuesdays which starts at 6.30pm with Ballet Floor-barre, followed by Improvers 2 Class & Elementary Pointe which comes with a saving of £35. (Repertoire will return in the Autumn term) • Place: Capital City College (Holloway), 444 Camden Road, London, N7 0SP • Dates: 15th September - 1st December 2026(11 lessons; no class 27 Oct) • Cost: £185 / £250 • Teacher: Serena Cundari

sessions.title: Advanced 1
sessions.details: Advanced 1 teaches complex steps and enchainements such as pirouettes in attitude, brisé volé, cabrioles and jeté elancé and is the ideal pre-cursor to Advanced 2 suiting higher skilled dancers. Classes are sometimes taken without the barre for added strength. Supporting videos are provided. Combine with Advanced 2 if more experienced and / or Improvers 2. Intermediate Pointe follows. • Place: New City Fitness, Redvers St, London, N1 6NE • Dates: 17th September - 3rd December 2026(11 lessons; no class 29 Oct) • Cost: £185 / £250 • Teacher: Serena Cundari

sessions.title: Advanced 2 (Saturday)
sessions.details: Advanced 2 is ideal for dancers at a high level of skill, professionals, returning dancers who are ballet-fit and vocational students who wish to work on strength, technique and developing their artistry. Variations en Pointe follows. Combine with Advanced 1 (which teaches many of the complex steps in Advanced 2) and Improvers 2. • Place: New City Fitness, Redvers St, London, N1 6NE • Dates: 19th September - 5th December 2026(11 lessons; no class 31 Oct) • Cost: £185 / £255 • Teacher: Serena Cundari

sessions.title: Tuesdays in Islington
sessions.details: Serena's Ballet Floor-Barre (BFB) consists of exercises based on Ballet steps performed lying and sitting on the floor. It's an ideal way to prepare and maintain the body for Ballet - strengthening, lengthening and toning, whilst increasing your turnout, flexibility and elasticity. It is a great complement to Ballet training. Some PBT (Progressing Ballet Technique) exercises using the small pilates ball (supplied) are included. • Place: Capital City College (Holloway), 444 Camden Road, London, N7 0SP15th September - 1st December 2026(11 lessons; no class 27 Oct) • Cost: £160 • Teacher: Serena CundariImprovers 2 follows at 7.35pm Save £35 when taken as part of the Tuesday Improvers 2 Discount Package

    Variations en Pointe is for Advanced Ladies and follows directly after their Advanced 2 Ballet class. • Place: New City Fitness, Redvers St, London, N1 6NE • Dates: 19th September - 5th December 2026(11 lessons; no class 31 Oct) • Cost: £70 • Teacher: Serena Cundari

sessions.title: Absolute Beginner Ballet
sessions.details: Mondays 6:30-7:30pm w/ Iole | This class is geared towards those who have either never taken ballet or are returning from a long hiatus. You will be (re)introduced to classical terminology and movement through a classical ballet barre giving you the opportunity to build core strength, mental and physical flexibility, musicality, and leg tone. Class will conclude with centre practice, which will offer you more freedom of movement and personal creativity. All in a warm, supportive, and welcoming environment.

sessions.title: Ballet Improver
sessions.details: Mondays 7:30-9:00pm w/ Iole | Ballet Improvers offers an open and relaxed atmosphere where dancers are encouraged to take part in discussion and reflection while deepening the foundations of ballet technique. Classes progress gradually, introducing new challenges and technical layers based on the group’s rhythm and development. Iole brings a personal touch to the ballet experience, focusing on expressiveness, musicality, coordination, and the connection between body and space. Great care is given to each dancer’s individual abilities and unique way of expressing the technique.

sessions.title: Adv/Pro Contemporary
sessions.details: Tuesdays 10:00-11:30am w/ Nadine | For professional and advanced level dancers, this contemporary class will have a released based focus with elements of Cunningham technique and inspiration taken from the commercial dance world.Class will follow a similar structure each week, starting with a Gyrokinesis inspired warm up, before moving through a variety of technical exercises to address the whole body before ending with a phrase. Aiming to prepare the body and mind for the day ahead.

sessions.title: Beginner Horton
sessions.details: Tuesdays 6:00-7:30pm w/ Melissa | Discover Horton Technique in this beginner-friendly class that builds dance from the ground up. Created by Lester Horton in the 1930s/40s, the technique develops alignment, strength, flexibility, and coordination—skills that support all styles of dance. We’ll focus on core movements like flat backs, laterals, and T positions, then progress to Horton’s signature fortifications and studies. With repetition, clear guidance, and supportive pacing, you’ll gain confidence, strength, and artistry.

sessions.title: Intro to Broadway Jazz
sessions.details: Tuesdays 7:30-8:30pm w/ Mette | A fun, high-energy musical theatre jazz class designed for complete beginners. Each class builds your confidence from the ground up, starting with a warm-up and learning individual jazz steps and techniques across the floor, before linking them together into a short routine to inspiring music. Explore rhythm, musicality and self-expression in a supportive, feel-good environment. No experience needed! Everything is learned in the room. ​ 07/07-28/07 Bring a friend and experience the joy of dance together | two for the price of one BOOK HERE

sessions.title: 'Bodies in Action' - Ballet for Contemporary Dancers
sessions.details: Wednesdays and Fridays 10:00-11:30am | This class is focused on creating an inclusive and positive space for professional contemporary dancers. Everybody is welcome to engage in our ballet for contemporary dancers company class without regard to gender identity, race, body type or disability; diversity is something we pride ourselves on. We aim to provide a class that will allow the body to become more aware of its physical activity and how we can further develop and hone in on our skills as contemporary dancers.

sessions.title: Contemporary Improver
sessions.details: Wednesdays 6:30-7:30pm w/ Iole | This class invites you to move, connect, and awaken rhythm through contemporary dance blending technique and exploration. Rooted in Cunningham, release, and floorwork, it weaves dynamic crossings, phrases, and guided improvisations shaped by Iole’s distinctive touch. You’ll play with momentum, texture, and flow, expanding coordination and expression. Open to movers with some experience, each session nurtures rhythm, body awareness, and freedom through a living dialogue of technique and artistry.

sessions.title: Beginner Contemporary
sessions.details: Wednesdays 7:30-8:30pm w/ Iole | This class invites you to explore movement and its connections, focusing on the principles and introduction to contemporary dance. Through a blend of exercises, crossings, and actions, we will explore the concepts of flow and momentum. This is an opportunity to build strength and rediscover the body's potential through movement. Classes will cover the fundamental concepts of the main modern and contemporary dance techniques, including release, floor work, Cunningham, and more.

sessions.title: General Level Ballet
sessions.details: Thursdays 7:00-8:30pm w/ Isla | This open-level class welcomes dancers of all backgrounds, whether you’re returning to ballet, building confidence at the barre, or looking to refine existing skills. Isla Roebig creates a supportive and encouraging environment, balancing strong technical foundations with musicality and flow.

sessions.title: Open Level Contemporary
sessions.details: Fridays 12:00-1:15pm w/ Chloe | Join Chloe Bowman for an energising and explorative contemporary class open to all levels. Grounded in release-based technique and guided improvisation, this class invites dancers to connect breath, weight, and flow through dynamic movement phrases. Chloe’s teaching emphasises musicality, individuality, and presence — creating a space where beginners feel supported and experienced movers can deepen their practice. Expect a thoughtful balance of structure and freedom, culminating in expansive, expressive choreography that celebrates movement in the moment.

sessions.title: Ballet Improver
sessions.details: Saturdays 12:00-1:00pm w/ Iole | Ballet Improvers offers an open and relaxed atmosphere where dancers are encouraged to take part in discussion and reflection while deepening the foundations of ballet technique. Classes progress gradually, introducing new challenges and technical layers based on the group’s rhythm and development. Iole brings a personal touch to the ballet experience, focusing on expressiveness, musicality, coordination, and the connection between body and space. Great care is given to each dancer’s individual abilities and unique way of expressing the technique.

sessions.title: Beginner Pointe (+Ballet Improver class prior)
sessions.details: Saturdays 1:15-1:45pm w/ Iole | Beginner Pointe is offered as a gentle extension of Ballet Improvers, designed for dancers who are ready to begin exploring pointe work in a supportive and attentive environment. This class introduces the fundamental principles of dancing en pointe at a gradual and thoughtful pace. Dancers will focus on correct alignment, strength, balance, and articulation of the feet, while deepening their understanding of placement and coordination developed in the improvers class. ​ Following the same open and reflective atmosphere, dancers are encouraged to listen closely to their bodies and progress with care and patience. Iole offers individual guidance to help each dancer build confidence, stability, and ease on pointe, while nurturing musicality and expressiveness. The emphasis is on developing strength safely, refining technique, and discovering the unique experience of dancing en pointe as a natural continuation of each dancer’s ballet journey.

sessions.title: Women-only Stretch Class
sessions.details: Laban Building • Creekside, London, SE8 3DZ, UK, London • This women-only stretch class is a highly requested add-on for anyone who wants a low-pressure space dedicated entirely to stretching. It is not yoga. Instead, it is a guided group fitness session built to improve flexibility, mobility and range of motion through focused, full-body stretching techniques. The class combines static stretching, dynamic movement and PNF-style contract-relax work, making it both restorative and functional. It is designed for people who want to carve out time to stretch properly, without rushing and without needing to fit it into a bigger dance or fitness class. The women-only environment adds an extra layer of comfort and relatability, making it easier to switch off, settle in and focus on your own body. By the end of the session, you should feel physically lighter, taller, more open and less stiff, with a calmer mind and a stronger sense of connection to your body. Because stretching time is often overlooked in busy schedules, this class creates a rare space where you can slow down, move with care, and leave feeling both released and re-energised. Who it’s for Women who want a calm, private, and comfortable space to stretch. Women who want to improve flexibility, mobility and range of motion. Women who prefer a guided stretch session rather than yoga. Women who want time to release tension and reset. What to expect You will move through a guided stretch and mobility session that combines careful, full-body work with space to breathe, release and settle into the room. The pace is controlled, supportive and focused on making the body feel more open and available. Every ticket includes a goody bag. Outcome You leave feeling looser, freer, more open through the body and more mentally settled. Practical note Please arrive 10–15 minutes early so you can settle in and get familiar with the space before class begins. If you are pregnant, postnatal, or managing an injury, please let the team know in advance so adjustments can be made. What to Wear Anything you feel comfortable in and able to stretch freely. This class is 18+ only. By purchasing a ticket, you agree to these terms and conditions.

sessions.title: Drop-In Tai Chi
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • In this Tai Chi practice, we will learn the Cheng Man Ching’s 37 Posture Short FormThe focus of this class is on the health vitality and meditation aspects of Tai Chi and you will gain an insight into the key principles, practice relaxation and breathing methods, body and mind alignment as well as have a greater understanding of why Tai Chi is beneficial to your overall physical and mental wellbeing.We will also make use of Qigong (Chi Kung) exercises which are an essential part of the Tai Chi discipline.This Tai Chi class in West London will help you feel calmer and more relaxed. Moreover, it will help increase your focus, concentration and vitality.All levels welcome!In this Tai Chi practice, we will learn the Cheng Man Ching’s 37 Posture Short FormThe focus of this class is on the health vitality and meditation aspects of Tai Chi and you will gain an insight into the key principles, practice relaxation and breathing methods, body and mind alignment as well as have a greater understanding of why Tai Chi is beneficial to your overall physical and mental wellbeing.We will also make use of Qigong (Chi Kung) exercises which are an essential part of the Tai Chi discipline.This Tai Chi class in West London will help you feel calmer and more relaxed. sessions.title: Human Garage Fascia Maneuver Workshop
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • Fascia Maneuvers by Human Garage Workshop – March Tuesday SeriesReady to unlock stuck trauma & energy from your body? With Human Garage Fascia Maneuvers, you can master a quiet mind and rejuvenated body!Fascial Maneuver Workshop- Guided by Human Garage Certified CoachJoin us for a restorative Fascia Maneuver Workshop — a guided journey of movement, breath, and awareness designed to release stored tension and trauma within the body’s connective tissue. Combining posture, breathwork, and mindful intention, this experience helps realign the body, expand mobility, and encourage emotional balance. Discover how gentle, self-applied techniques can unlock your body’s natural flow and restore harmony from the inside out.What are Fascia Maneuvers?Fascial Maneuvers, created by Human Garage, are self-applied movements & breathwork techniques that release tension and trauma stored in the fascia—the connective tissue around muscles and organs. sessions.title: Drum Circle (6-session series)
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • Get ready to drum up some rhythm at our Drum Circle event! Immerse yourself in a beat-packed 6-session series – whether you're in for the whole groove with full-course tickets or feel like jamming spontaneously with our drop-in tickets. Let's keep the vibe rhythmic!Alternate Thursdays 19:30 - 21:00, West Studio (Apr 30th - Jul 9th)Our African Drum Circle workshop in London gives you the chance to learn to play the African “Djembe” Drum. Explore the “bass, tone, slap” sounds of this instrument and discover why African drumming is irresistible.Drumming is a great way to release stress and develop creativity, and it’s also the oldest form of communal musical bonding. The class will also include time to dance, shake and move to the beat.Drums are provided. No experience needed!With Ailon & KayKay SmithI have been playing djembe for 15 years and love to perform and teach. I like to describe my drum circle sessions as an introduction to the Joys of Djembe. I love what I do and it makes me happy to be able to share my passion with others. My goal is to create an enjoyable experience for all levels and experience of participants.Ailon FreeFor the last 30 years, Ailon’s professional life has been devoted to the synthesis of spiritual practice with creative self expression. In 2001, he set up The Lotus Exchange -London’s first corporate yoga service, and since then he has worked as a yoga teacher, bodyworker and corporate facilitator as well as an artist, comedian, clown and drumming teacher.Get ready to drum up some rhythm at our Drum Circle event! sessions.title: The Yoga of Vocal Sound, Music and Meditation
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • Are you ready to embark on a journey of self-transformation? Join James D’Angelo – a leading authority on sound healing – in uncovering one of the great aspects of attaining true sound health through the tuning of the chakras.In this unique workshop, James will introduce you to vocal homeopathy, and its processes by which the chakras are stimulated and re-tuned, clearing blockages and releasing fine energies. Alongside the therapeutic use of voice, he will bring in embodiment practices with Chi Kung-like movements integrating them into the toning practices. Finally, and complementary to the chakra work, you will experience samples of Taoist healing sounds/movements. Learning mantras for the subtle energy field, and drawing vocal sound within as a form of meditation.No previous experience is necessary, only a deep intent to be your true Self.James D’Angelo has been leading his Soundspirit courses in the UK, Europe and the USA since 1996 and is considered a leading authority on sound healing practices. He is the author of The Healing Power of the Human Voice and Seed Sounds for Tuning the Chakras: Vowels, Consonants and Seed Syllables for Spiritual Transformation. www.soundspirit.co.ukAre you ready to embark on a journey of self-transformation? Discover how gentle, self-applied techniques can unlock your body’s natural flow and restore harmony from the inside out.What are Fascia Maneuvers?Fascial Maneuvers, created by Human Garage, are self-applied movements & breathwork techniques that release tension and trauma stored in the fascia—the connective tissue around muscles and organs. Combining posture, breath, intention, motion, they restore alignment, mobility, emotional balance.Why participate in this Fascia Maneuver workshop?Release built-up tension and trauma held in the body’s connective tissues, restore natural alignment, and support emotional release and flow.What to expect?Participants will practice guided self-movements, breathwork, posture shifts, and intention to gradually unlock fascial restrictions and expand mobility in a safe, supportive setting. PLEASE BRING A YOGA MAT, WATER and Curious Mind!Precautions and Limitations:Not suitable during acute injury, open wounds, deep vein thrombosis, unstable spine, or for those with conditions such as uncontrolled hypertension or severe osteoporosis. Please consult your healthcare provider if pregnant or managing serious health issues.

sessions.title: Playfulness for Beginners (4-week course)
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • 7.15-9.00pm on Wednesdays June 3rd, 10th, 17th and 24thWho says your spiritual practice needs to be serious? What if playfulness could become a pathway towards self-discovery and wholeness?Come and join us for this 4-week introductory ‘practice of playfulness' journey, where grown up adults can reconnect with their often buried sense of childlike play. We will draw from the worlds of clowning, improv, mime and dance as well as spontaneous silliness and generalised irreverence. Together, this unlikely cocktail will leave you feeling joyous, open-hearted and flooded with endorphins.Through a range of games and structures, we will explore aspects of play such as spontaneity, misbehaviour, exuberance, sensitivity, connection and totality - and also take time to explore and process how we are feeling as we undergo the journey.The workshops will be a safe space with no pressure ‘to perform’- although the urge may arise. Rather the aim is to build an encouraging supportive nest from which you can launch yourself into your innately exuberant and playful aspect and free your inner child.Some aspects of the workshop will be held and structured, others will be a little bit free and spontaneous (and even occasionally anarchic), all interspersed with dancing and 'digestion time.' There will also be a chill-out area should you need to sit things out for a while ************* During this course you will: Discover how The Practice of Playfulness is a healing medicine that can transform emotions such as repression, numbness and disconnection into feelings of happiness, optimism and inner aliveness.See how the path of self-expression develops inner qualities such as courage, creativity, confidence and improved communication skills.Understand how play gives a strong taste of emotional freedom and moment-by-moment living in a real, embodied way.Be supported through regular sharing circles and times for self-reflection. ************* Read other people's experience of the course:“I believe that what Ailon is offering is incredibly powerful and if you want to live life with a bit more lightheartedness then you should definitely go along to his workshops!”“Thank you for an enriching and ridiculous 6 weeks of playfulness.I loved the journey and cultivating the trust to play together allowed fresh and unexpected expressions to emerge.”“For me this is less of a class or workshop and more of a happening…a congregation of joyful souls happy to hang out and relax in a climate of friendliness. Out of that trust, true silliness and fun can emerge.”“Thank you for bringing into fruition a space which is so playful and nourishing to the soul. To have all our parts to come up and be met, not only with with encouragement but with love is so rare in this world- it takes weird and deep to awaken and meet weird and deep!”“ It’s been a pleasure being on this journey with every single one of you and see everyone expand and grow on this journey into deeper expression of our selves. I appreciate everyone’s acceptance and kindness. It’s been the first place in a long time where I felt safe and accepted to just be myself and explore parts of myself no matter how crazy, silly and unexpected.”"Ailon creates such a safe and humorous space for people to explore their inner joker and let go of their inhibitions. I’ve given myself permission to be playful again and it’s been so liberating to reconnect with that part of me."“What a pleasure and a privilege to be part of this workshop” “Like being drunk with no alcohol”****************They can't all be wrong ???Any questions email: ailon@schoolofplayfulness.co.ukTo read more about Ailon (the founder)'s journey into playfulness have a look herehttps://ailonfreedman.substack.com/p/birthing-the-school-of-playfulness7.15-9.00pm on Wednesdays June 3rd, 10th, 17th and 24thWho says your spiritual practice needs to be serious? sessions.title: 5Rhythms with Flow
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • In 5Rhythms, we hand over prayer to the body, allowing it to become our wisest teacher. Through movement, we invite the body to express its unique rhythm in the present moment, uncovering its often suppressed intelligence.Flow Vulk brings her own energy and vision to the dance. In her words:"I found my feet on a 5Rhythms dance floor in 2014. This is when and where it all magnificently fell apart before falling into place and making sense. It is in our bones, blood and breath - the beat, the rhythm, the Song of Songs. The Song of Life. Dance it. This is how we wake up, how we become alive again, how we find answers and resolutions. We dance not to forget but to remember, not to get lost but to find ourselves, not to disappear but to arise in our full, authentic glory. So let this be an open invitation to shake off the cobwebs, rattle those bones, let that breath flow and let your feet find their beat. Let Life In."All are welcome, whether you’re new to 5Rhythms or have danced it for years.In 5Rhythms, we hand over prayer to the body, allowing it to become our wisest teacher. Through movement, we invite the body to express its unique rhythm in the present moment, uncovering its often suppressed intelligence.Flow Vulk brings her own energy and vision to the dance. In her words:"I found my feet on a 5Rhythms dance floor in 2014. This is when and where it all magnificently fell apart before falling into place and making sense. It is in our bones, blood and breath - the beat, the rhythm, the Song of Songs. The Song of Life. Dance it. This is how we wake up, how we become alive again, how we find answers and resolutions. We dance not to forget but to remember, not to get lost but to find ourselves, not to disappear but to arise in our full, authentic glory. So let this be an open invitation to shake off the cobwebs, rattle those bones, let that breath flow and let your feet find their beat. Let Life In."All are welcome, whether you’re new to 5Rhythms or have danced it for years.

sessions.title: Baduanjin Qigong (8-week course)
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • BADUANJIN QIGONG – The Eight Silk BrocadesAn Eight-Week Qigong Course with Natalia Miralles Huete📅 Course dates: 8 classes from 9 April to 11 June (No class on 23 April and 4 June)18:30 – 19:30📍 Location: Top Studio, Colet HouseBaduanjin, also known as “The Eight Silk Brocades”, is an ancient Qigong set of eight different fluid movements that together condition the whole body externally — stretching and strengthening it — and internally, improving the function of the organs and systems (respiratory, circulatory, digestive, nervous, etc.).The set was first described in an eighth-century Taoist text, and it is known to have been used effectively throughout the 17th century during wars in China to alleviate soldiers’ fatigue. This is a key aspect of Qigong, as it helps us to regain energy and vitality to go about our modern daily lives and face our inner battles of stress and emotional challenges.Baduanjin represents an excellent short daily health routine. It will provide you with a whole-body workout, mobilising all your joints, strengthening your muscles and bones, and improving your posture and balance, while helping to focus and calm your busy mind. Your emotions will also feel more balanced, and your energy levels will increase.Once you have learnt the set, it will take you just 20 minutes to complete, although the practice can be shorter or longer depending on the number of repetitions. If you persevere in practising the set daily or a few times per week after the course, you will soon notice the health benefits it brings.The course also includes the essential foundations for effective Qigong practice: breathing techniques, preparatory self-massage, structured joint-opening exercises, and the “three regulations” — body, breath and mind — providing a clear and methodical framework for practising Baduanjin safely and efficiently.BADUANJIN QIGONG – The Eight Silk BrocadesAn Eight-Week Qigong Course with Natalia Miralles Huete📅 Course dates: 8 classes from 9 April to 11 June (No class on 23 April and 4 June)18:30 – 19:30📍 Location: Top Studio, Colet HouseBaduanjin, also known as “The Eight Silk Brocades”, is an ancient Qigong set of eight different fluid movements that together condition the whole body externally — stretching and strengthening it — and internally, improving the function of the organs and systems (respiratory, circulatory, digestive, nervous, etc.).The set was first described in an eighth-century Taoist text, and it is known to have been used effectively throughout the 17th century during wars in China to alleviate soldiers’ fatigue. This is a key aspect of Qigong, as it helps us to regain energy and vitality to go about our modern daily lives and face our inner battles of stress and emotional challenges.Baduanjin represents an excellent short daily health routine. It will provide you with a whole-body workout, mobilising all your joints, strengthening your muscles and bones, and improving your posture and balance, while helping to focus and calm your busy mind. Your emotions will also feel more balanced, and your energy levels will increase.Once you have learnt the set, it will take you just 20 minutes to complete, although the practice can be shorter or longer depending on the number of repetitions. If you persevere in practising the set daily or a few times per week after the course, you will soon notice the health benefits it brings.The course also includes the essential foundations for effective Qigong practice: breathing techniques, preparatory self-massage, structured joint-opening exercises, and the “three regulations” — body, breath and mind — providing a clear and methodical framework for practising Baduanjin safely and efficiently.

sessions.title: The Yoga of Vocal Sound, Music and Meditation
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • Are you ready to embark on a journey of self-transformation? Join James D’Angelo – a leading authority on sound healing – in uncovering one of the great aspects of attaining true sound health through the tuning of the chakras.In this unique workshop, James will introduce you to vocal homeopathy, and its processes by which the chakras are stimulated and re-tuned, clearing blockages and releasing fine energies. Alongside the therapeutic use of voice, he will bring in embodiment practices with Chi Kung-like movements integrating them into the toning practices. Finally, and complementary to the chakra work, you will experience samples of Taoist healing sounds/movements. Learning mantras for the subtle energy field, and drawing vocal sound within as a form of meditation.No previous experience is necessary, only a deep intent to be your true Self.James D’Angelo has been leading his Soundspirit courses in the UK, Europe and the USA since 1996 and is considered a leading authority on sound healing practices. He is the author of The Healing Power of the Human Voice and Seed Sounds for Tuning the Chakras: Vowels, Consonants and Seed Syllables for Spiritual Transformation. www.soundspirit.co.ukAre you ready to embark on a journey of self-transformation? Join James D’Angelo – a leading authority on sound healing – in uncovering one of the great aspects of attaining true sound health through the tuning of the chakras.In this unique workshop, James will introduce you to vocal homeopathy, and its processes by which the chakras are stimulated and re-tuned, clearing blockages and releasing fine energies. sessions.title: The Movements
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • Meditation through movementThe Movements are a collection of sacred dances, exercises, prayers and their accompanying music that originated from various schools, dervish orders and monasteries across the Middle East and Central Asia. They were collected and brought to the West by the philosopher and mystic, G.I. Gurdjieff. They were first taught in England to Ouspensky’s pupils at Lyne Place and Colet House in the 1930s. The Study Society is unique in teaching the early repertoire of Movements as they were originally taught, in an unchanged line to the present day.The Movements provide a non-verbal way of experiencing the essence of the Fourth Way teaching. They convey knowledge about the great universal laws and can be a vehicle for expressing universal truths. They can be thought of as a special form of ‘moving meditation’. Through the practice of attention they can enable participants to experience the working of finer energies, bringing all parts of the human organism into a more harmonious whole.The Study Society offers these evenings free of charge, but you are invited to make a donation to support the charity in its works.Meditation through movementThe Movements are a collection of sacred dances, exercises, prayers and their accompanying music that originated from various schools, dervish orders and monasteries across the Middle East and Central Asia. sessions.title: Drum Circle (6-session series)
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • Get ready to drum up some rhythm at our Drum Circle event! Immerse yourself in a beat-packed 6-session series – whether you're in for the whole groove with full-course tickets or feel like jamming spontaneously with our drop-in tickets. Let's keep the vibe rhythmic!Alternate Thursdays 19:30 - 21:00, West Studio (Jan 22nd - Apr 2nd)Our African Drum Circle workshop in London gives you the chance to learn to play the African “Djembe” Drum. Explore the “bass, tone, slap” sounds of this instrument and discover why African drumming is irresistible.Drumming is a great way to release stress and develop creativity, and it’s also the oldest form of communal musical bonding. The class will also include time to dance, shake and move to the beat.Drums are provided. No experience needed!With Ailon & KayKay SmithI have been playing djembe for 15 years and love to perform and teach. I like to describe my drum circle sessions as an introduction to the Joys of Djembe. I love what I do and it makes me happy to be able to share my passion with others. My goal is to create an enjoyable experience for all levels and experience of participants.Ailon FreeFor the last 30 years, Ailon’s professional life has been devoted to the synthesis of spiritual practice with creative self expression. In 2001, he set up The Lotus Exchange -London’s first corporate yoga service, and since then he has worked as a yoga teacher, bodyworker and corporate facilitator as well as an artist, comedian, clown and drumming teacher.Get ready to drum up some rhythm at our Drum Circle event! sessions.title: Wednesday Open Class & Jam ON THE HEATH
sessions.details: Hampstead Heath, London • This week, we will be moving the Wednesday Open Class & Jam out onto Hampstead Heath for the last class of the term because of the forecast hot weather. The course is a carefully crafted syllabus that will support beginners in understanding the conceptual and physical world of Contact Improvisation (CI) so that they can trust their own opinions about what they want as they dive into the CI experience. Connecting with other people is at the core of this practice and you will learn how CI roots connection in the reality of touch and movement. Class 1) A talk about what CI is and what to expect from it, a lead somatic warm-up and a solo physical exploration about moving comfortably in close connection with the floor, exercises about communicating with a partner through touch and physical listening, discussion of consensual touch and freedom of choice in dancing, a space for questions and concerns to be expressed and addressed. Class 2) We will learn some simple patterns that open different body surfaces to meeting in contact. Exercises that clarify how to get in and out of dances will strengthen your agency in following your interests within a group. Class 3) Exercises about exchanging body weight with a partner will be the beginning of the thrill of taking risks, understanding that good communication leads to pleasure in weight exchange and a lack of communication leads to discomfort, feeling your strength and understanding your limits. Class 4) Exercises about how to fall safely, learning more CI vocabulary to expand possibilities in physical communication, building confidence in starting and ending dances. Class 5) Understanding Jamming and how to decode its rules and etiquette, responses to specific questions and a discussion about transitioning to open classes and jams. Integrating previous learning into free dancing and connecting to fun and risk in movement. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sis0w-20260427183000

sessions.title: Saturday CI Workshop: Performing Oneself
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • This workshop is about being an audience and having an audience. Showing up and witnessing. Seeing and being seen, being yourself and being in a group. The focus will not be on looking good but rather on the way in which being seen supports us in seeing ourselves. We will frame witnessing each other dance as an expanded way of connecting. We will investigate relating to multiple partners at the same time and getting in and out of contact with a partner while maintaining the integrity of our own dance. We will move on to ensemble scores for improvising together. We will look at turning our own desires into action and how these individual expressions of creativity can coexist in a shared space with others. Issues or blocks related to fear, failure, taking space and confusion will be welcomed and fuel the training of our agency and awareness. As always with my workshops, you can choose your own level of challenge. Technical study will evolve into free Contact Dancing, where we can explore and integrate new learnings into improvisation. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sn3d0-20260516143000

sessions.title: Saturday CI x BMC® Workshop with Rick Nodine & Aki Omori: Moving from Within
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • This workshop introduces primitive reflexes, early developmental movement, and the organ system through the lens of Body-Mind Centering®. We will explore how these may support more fluid, responsive interactions in Contact Improvisation and expand our movement language with others, allowing expression to arise from within. We will start with a whole-body pattern to sense what an integrated, connected body feels like. From there, we will shift our attention to the soft core, investigating pathways for sequencing energy through the entire body, aligning inner experience with outward expression. This work is playful, intimate and engaging - fostering awareness, receptivity and enhanced responsiveness in Contact Improvisation. Photo by Lenka Rayn H https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-s6tun-20260523143000

sessions.title: Monday Beginners Level 1 (Course of 5 classes)
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • Mondays 27/04, 04/05, 11/05, 18/05 and 25/05 from 6:30 to 8:30pm. Classes taught by Rick Nodine. The course is a carefully crafted syllabus that will support beginners in understanding the conceptual and physical world of Contact Improvisation (CI) so that they can trust their own opinions about what they want as they dive into the CI experience. Connecting with other people is at the core of this practice and you will learn how CI roots connection in the reality of touch and movement. Class 1) A talk about what CI is and what to expect from it, a lead somatic warm-up and a solo physical exploration about moving comfortably in close connection with the floor, exercises about communicating with a partner through touch and physical listening, discussion of consensual touch and freedom of choice in dancing, a space for questions and concerns to be expressed and addressed. Class 2) We will learn some simple patterns that open different body surfaces to meeting in contact. Exercises that clarify how to get in and out of dances will strengthen your agency in following your interests within a group. Class 3) Exercises about exchanging body weight with a partner will be the beginning of the thrill of taking risks, understanding that good communication leads to pleasure in weight exchange and a lack of communication leads to discomfort, feeling your strength and understanding your limits. Class 4) Exercises about how to fall safely, learning more CI vocabulary to expand possibilities in physical communication, building confidence in starting and ending dances. Class 5) Understanding Jamming and how to decode its rules and etiquette, responses to specific questions and a discussion about transitioning to open classes and jams. Integrating previous learning into free dancing and connecting to fun and risk in movement. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-srouz-20260525183000

sessions.title: Saturday CI x Aikido Workshop with Rosalie Wahlfrid: The Power of Letting Go
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • In this workshop, we will explore the potential of the quiet body. Through movement and meditative practices, we will investigate how qualities such as softening, absorbing and deflecting may generate power, efficiency and responsiveness in movement. The workshop will introduce simple Aikido techniques alongside the fundamentals of posture and embodiment in Aikido and explore physical and mental states such as spatial awareness and internal focus. We will consider how these principles and techniques may support more fluid and responsive interactions in Contact Improvisation, while expanding our shared movement vocabulary. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sfc76-20260606143000

Beginners 1: The course is a carefully crafted syllabus that will support beginners in understanding the conceptual and physical world of Contact Improvisation (CI) so that they can trust their own opinions about what they want as they dive into the CI experience. Connecting with other people is at the core of this practice and you will learn how CI roots connection in the reality of touch and movement. Class 1) A talk about what CI is and what to expect from it, a lead somatic warm-up and a solo physical exploration about moving comfortably in close connection with the floor, exercises about communicating with a partner through touch and physical listening, discussion of consensual touch and freedom of choice in dancing, a space for questions and concerns to be expressed and addressed. Class 2) We will learn some simple patterns that open different body surfaces to meeting in contact. Exercises that clarify how to get in and out of dances will strengthen your agency in following your interests within a group. Class 3) Exercises about exchanging body weight with a partner will be the beginning of the thrill of taking risks, understanding that good communication leads to pleasure in weight exchange and a lack of communication leads to discomfort, feeling your strength and understanding your limits. Class 4) Exercises about how to fall safely, learning more CI vocabulary to expand possibilities in physical communication, building confidence in starting and ending dances. Class 5) Understanding Jamming and how to decode its rules and etiquette, responses to specific questions and a discussion about transitioning to open classes and jams. Integrating previous learning into free dancing and connecting to fun and risk in movement. Beginners 2: It will build on what we started in Beginners 1, deepening your understanding of the basics and developing more vocabulary for giving and receiving weight, moving in close connection with the floor, rolling and falling. We will work on expanding confidence in: Expressing what you want and do not want in your partner dances Creativity and playfulness Starting and finishing dances Deep physical listening Dancing in trios Lifting and being lifted Jamming skills I will be working with new material, so it will be a way of revisiting the fundamentals while learning new pathways. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-smfda-20260629183000

sessions.title: Wednesday Open Class & Jam ON THE HEATH
sessions.details: Hampstead Heath, London • This week, we will be moving the Wednesday Open Class & Jam out onto Hampstead Heath for the last class of the term because of the forecast hot weather. We will meet and dance on the field behind the Lido, close to Gospel Oak station. A somatic warm up will focus on connecting body to earth and sky. Dress for rolling in the grass. Bring food & drinks and we will picnic and hang out together after class to celebrate the arrival of summer and part in style. sessions.title: Summer CI retreat with Rick Nodine, Tender Agility, Gaia Terra
sessions.details: Gaia Terra, Via Francesco Petrarca, 45, Rivignano Teor, UD, 33061 • It is called Tender Agility, and it's all in the name: tender: /ˈtɛndə/ 1. showing kindness and affection agility: /əˈdʒɪlɪti/ 1. ability to move quickly and easily It will be a 6 day CI intensive (24-30 August 2026) in the North of Italy, with workshops, bodywork and somatics sessions, Jams, hang out in the sun and swim in the river. We have been wanting to collaborate with Gaia Terra for a long time, so we are excited about spending a week in this Ecovillage nested in beautiful countryside, equipped with a state-of-the-art dance studio and gardens where they grow the food we will eat. CI Workshops will include: Somatic exploration CI technique/patterns and skills Exploration space Where? Gaia Terra, in the North of Italy. Gaia Terra is an Ecovillage nested in beautiful nature equipped with a state-of-the-art dance studio, beautiful surroundings and a river! Gaia Terra is more than just a site that is hosting us. It is an experience that aligns with the values of our community. With the values we aim to support about sharing, caring, growth and respect for nature. All participants will be asked to become a member of Gaia Terra (EUR 5 membership fee to pay upon arrival) and to contribute to the life of the Ecovillage with two 30 minutes slots of volunteering during their stay. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-smmkt-20260824000000

sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • 4 teachers, 4 unique perspectives on dancing through the lenses of Choreography, Dancing through the camera, Group Process and Contact Improvisation. sessions.title: Bound & Unbound Weekend, Autumn Edition: Exploring the Edges of Contact Improvisation
sessions.details: Anatomie Studio, Arch 113 Unit 17 Station Passage, London, SE15 2JR • A three day intensive with Jamus Wood & Rick Nodine. Contact Improvisation, Shibari & BMC®. In this short Intensive we will take time to explore some of the territory at the edges of Contact Improvisation. Exploring aspects of the dance that often go unrecognised or don’t receive quality attention. Contact improvisation, led by Rick, will be at the heart of our practice and the ground of our shared language. We will spend the first part of our time together dropping into inner and outer awareness through Body Mind Centering® exercises and perspectives. The intention is to bring a felt sense of embodiment and a deep listening to our dance. Later we will draw inspiration from Shibari (Japanese Rope Play) with Jamus. Initially, we’ll focus on the sensations of our bodies in relation to one another, without ropes yet through physical limitations, exploring binding and unbinding, being held and being free. We will then introduce ropes, engaging in Ichinawa (one rope technique) to explore further the qualities of resistance and surrender, stillness and breath, gravity and flow. A key aspect of our workshop will be The Switch —a playful exploration of roles as we transition between being bound and binding with one’s body. We will slow down and zoom in, lingering in each role for some time. Also taking moments when neither of us are in a role. We will emphasise consent throughout our time together. Consent will be taught as a practice so that we can dance within our limits while finding and exploring our edges together. We’ll conclude each day with an exploratory jam, allowing space to integrate what we've explored and to express ourselves freely, weaving our individual journeys into a collective tapestry of creativity and joy. Join us in this supportive circle of explorers. Together, let’s celebrate the borders of Contact Improvisation with Shibari, deepening our connections through movement, trust and shared discovery. Limited tickets available https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-stf0y-20261016183000

Consent will be taught as a practice so that we can dance within our limits while finding and exploring our edges together. We’ll conclude each day with an exploratory jam, allowing space to integrate what we've explored and to express ourselves freely, weaving our individual journeys into a collective tapestry of creativity and joy. Join us in this supportive circle of explorers. Together, let’s celebrate the borders of Contact Improvisation with Shibari, deepening our connections through movement, trust and shared discovery. Limited tickets available https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-s92gv-20261018100000

sessions.title: Contact Improvisation Intensive With Rick Nodine: Training Ground, Autumn 26 Edition
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • London, Thursday October 29th to Sunday November 01st, 10h-17h In this intensive 4 days of study we will explore the ground that CI dances on. We will build our connect to earth, through the floor, with solo Somatics and simple but essential patterns that underpin the CI body. We will grow confidence in falling, rolling and inverting ourselves so that our partner dances can expand beyond habitual limits. Extended contact dancing guided by scores that demand connection through principles will guide us to the edges we need to work at. We will also reclaim the floor as playground for fresh explorations of the form and the pleasures of improvisation for its own sake. Time to explore, space to discover and days to build new skills will lead to questions and some answers about the dances you are looking for. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-shsgr-20261029100000

sessions.title: Monday Beginners Level 1+2 (Course of 12 classes)
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • Mondays 07/09, 14/09, 21/09, 28/09, 05/10 and 12/10 (Beginners 1) And Mondays 02/11, 09/11, 16/11, 23/11, 30/11 and 07/12 (Beginners 2) from 6:30 to 8:30pm. Classes taught by Rick Nodine. Beginners 1: The course is a carefully crafted syllabus that will support beginners in understanding the conceptual and physical world of Contact Improvisation (CI) so that they can trust their own opinions about what they want as they dive into the CI experience. Connecting with other people is at the core of this practice and you will learn how CI roots connection in the reality of touch and movement. Class 1) A talk about what CI is and what to expect from it, a lead somatic warm-up and a solo physical exploration about moving comfortably in close connection with the floor, exercises about communicating with a partner through touch and physical listening, discussion of consensual touch and freedom of choice in dancing, a space for questions and concerns to be expressed and addressed. Class 2) We will learn some simple patterns that open different body surfaces to meeting in contact. Exercises that clarify how to get in and out of dances will strengthen your agency in following your interests within a group. Class 3) Exercises about exchanging body weight with a partner will be the beginning of the thrill of taking risks, understanding that good communication leads to pleasure in weight exchange and a lack of communication leads to discomfort, feeling your strength and understanding your limits. Class 4) Exercises about how to fall safely, learning more CI vocabulary to expand possibilities in physical communication, building confidence in starting and ending dances. Class 5) Exercises about how to fall safely, learning more CI vocabulary to expand possibilities in physical communication, building confidence in starting and ending dances. Class 6) Understanding Jamming and how to decode its rules and etiquette, responses to specific questions and a discussion about transitioning to open classes and jams. Integrating previous learning into free dancing and connecting to fun and risk in movement. Beginners 2: This course will build on what we started in Beginners 1, deepening your understanding of the basics and developing more vocabulary for giving and receiving weight, moving in close connection with the floor, rolling and falling. We will work on expanding confidence in: Expressing what you want and do not want in your partner dances Creativity and playfulness Starting and finishing dances Deep physical listening Dancing in trios Lifting and being lifted Jamming skills I will be working with new material, so it will be a way of revisiting the fundamentals while learning new pathways. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sc3xz-20261102183000

sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • Mondays 02/11, 09/11, 16/11, 23/11, 30/11 and 07/12 from 6:30 to 8:30pm. Classes taught by Rick Nodine. We will work on expanding confidence in: Expressing what you want and do not want in your partner dances. Creativity and playfulness. Starting and finishing dances. Deep physical listening. Dancing in trios Lifting and being lifted Jamming skills https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-syjnd-20261102183000

Class 3) Exercises about exchanging body weight with a partner will be the beginning of the thrill of taking risks, understanding that good communication leads to pleasure in weight exchange and a lack of communication leads to discomfort, feeling your strength and understanding your limits. Class 4) Exercises about how to fall safely, learning more CI vocabulary to expand possibilities in physical communication, building confidence in starting and ending dances. Class 5) Exercises about how to fall safely, learning more CI vocabulary to expand possibilities in physical communication, building confidence in starting and ending dances. Class 6) Understanding Jamming and how to decode its rules and etiquette, responses to specific questions and a discussion about transitioning to open classes and jams. Integrating previous learning into free dancing and connecting to fun and risk in movement. Beginners 2: This course will build on what we started in Beginners 1, deepening your understanding of the basics and developing more vocabulary for giving and receiving weight, moving in close connection with the floor, rolling and falling. We will work on expanding confidence in: Expressing what you want and do not want in your partner dances Creativity and playfulness Starting and finishing dances Deep physical listening Dancing in trios Lifting and being lifted Jamming skills I will be working with new material, so it will be a way of revisiting the fundamentals while learning new pathways. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sc3xz-20261130183000

Connecting with other people is at the core of this practice and you will learn how CI roots connection in the reality of touch and movement. Class 1) A talk about what CI is and what to expect from it, a lead somatic warm-up and a solo physical exploration about moving comfortably in close connection with the floor, exercises about communicating with a partner through touch and physical listening, discussion of consensual touch and freedom of choice in dancing, a space for questions and concerns to be expressed and addressed.
