sessions.title: "SENSATIONAL MOVES": 9 -12 April . 4 DAY INTENSIVE Feldenkrais, Movement Exploration & CI with Simonetta Alessandri & Thomas Kampe
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • 9 -12 April 4 day Intensive exploring somatic dance practice, Feldenkrais Method and Contact Improvisation. In this four-day workshop, we combine somatic movement principles of the Feldenkrais Method® with dance exploration & Contact Improvisation practice. In the morning sessions we will be using ‘Awareness Through Movement’® lessons and touch-based explorations. We will focus on solo explorations and will connect with our core and imagination, deepening our felt sense of gravity and grounding. Through Dance Improvisational practice we will develop our observation skills and individual creative agency to integrate our embodied discoveries, fostering creative risk-taking and playfulness. In the afternoon sessions we will expand these explorations into the practice of Contact Improvisation to enhance our ability to interact and communicate expressively and playfully with ease and clarity. Drawing on our felt sense and somatic attunement through touch and gravity, we will revisit and refine Contact Improvisation skills and creative vocabularies, such as weight exchange, balance, support and lifts with a partner and in small ensemble situations. Each day we will take time to discuss or write reflectively about our personal and somatic discoveries. The Feldenkrais Method®, developed by Dr. Moshe Feldenkrais (1904-1984) is a gentle, embodied process focused on self-discovery, embodied thinking, creative wellbeing, and functional improvement. It invites participants to slow down, listen to internal sensations, and create new neural pathways that support creative choice making in action. This workshop is open to anyone interested in deepening their dance or movement practice. Thomas and Simonetta have been teaching Feldenkrais-informed dance classes around the globe for over 20 years and are world-leading researchers and artists in the field. Dr.Thomas Kampe (PhD) (he/him) has worked as a performer, artist, researcher and somatic educator across the globe for 40 years and was ‘Professor of Somatic Performance and Education’ at Bath Spa University (UK) between 2012 and 2022. Thomas initially trained in visual art and dance and is a teacher of the Feldenkrais Method ® which forms a rich foundation for his teaching, arts practice and research. He is interested in critical somatic arts legacies and his writings and performance works have been published internationally. He was editor of the Feldenkrais Research Journal Vol. 6 ‘Practices of Freedom: The Feldenkrais Method & Creativity’ in 2019, and recently co-edited JDSP Vol. 13.1 & 2 (2022): ‘Embodying Eco-Consciousness: Somatics, Aesthetic Practice and Social Action’. Simonetta Alessandri is an Italian dance artist & somatic educator based in London. She applies the Feldenkrais Method in dance & movement training and in performance making. She teaches at Trinity Laban, Goldsmiths University and London Contemporary Dance School. Her work is informed by more than 30 years of dancing, teaching and choreographing. Her choreography has been for dance companies, student pieces, large scale opera, improvised performance, site specific and movement direction for theatre. She was one of the few dance practitioners in Italy who began working with CI in the 90’s. She has been a guest teacher in Germany, Colombia, UK, Norway, Israel, France, Switzerland, Italy, Poland, Spain and Taiwan. She obtained the Post Graduate Diploma at London Contemporary Dance School; she is a qualified teacher of the Feldenkrais Method and she holds the Teacher Certificate of the Royal Academy of Dance. She is a founder member of CI@Goldsmiths London. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-swh5j-20260409100000

sessions.title: 'From Floor to Flying' CI Class with Josh Jones & Jam Soundscape from Michael Sebastian DROP IN FOR THE JAM ALWAYS AVAILABLE
sessions.details: Studio 3, Richard Hoggart Building, London, Se14 6nw • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00. From Floor to Flying This 2-hour class explores the relationship between floor and sky through contemporary floorwork and contact improvisation. We begin with attunement - arriving into body, space, and shared awareness. Through head-tail connectivity, spirals, yielding, and momentum, we develop an intelligent relationship with the ground. In my approach, the floor is our first partner. What we learn here - how to pour weight, how to trust support, how to yield - translates directly into dancing with another body. In flight and base relationships, your base becomes your floor. Through their support, you can feel the earth again. We work with pouring weight rather than placing it - allowing time for invitations to be sensed and received. Instead of making something happen, we practice allowing something to emerge. Invitations move both ways, and through listening and timing we find shared consent in motion. Open to experienced movers and confident beginners comfortable with weight and level changes. BIO Josh Jones is a movement educator and facilitator working at the meeting point of floorwork, somatics, and contact improvisation. With a background in contemporary dance training and years of immersion in CI communities, festivals, and research spaces internationally, his practice bridges technical precision with embodied listening. He has studied release-based floorwork, partnering, and somatic approaches that inform his progressive pathway from ground to flight. Josh has led workshops, retreats, and intensives across the UK, Europe, and India, supporting movers in developing clarity, adaptability, and trust in shared weight. He is the creator of the Lean In projects - including Lean In Nature and From Floor to Flying - immersive training spaces that explore the floor as our first partner, cultivating skill, sensitivity, and human connection through movement. See the website for more details on our pricing tiers. NO CASH ACCEPTED ON THE DOOR QR code to a payment link will be on display Drop-in jam price: £12 Regular/£10 Concession TaP Goldsmiths Students contact Simonetta Alessandri - s.alessandri@gold.co.uk https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-sl2vw-20260419130000

sessions.title: 'Meeting, Connecting ,Playing' Contact Improvisation WORKSHOP with Lucia Walker
sessions.details: Studio 3, Richard Hoggart Building, London, Se14 6nw • Meeting, Connecting ,Playing Meeting yourself, other dancers and the physical forces of this planet with more simplicity and depth. Connecting with more ease Playing with more presence, consent and freedom. Balancing the excitement of moving and connecting with the calm of receptivity and co-ordination. Working with directing our attention we will continue to refine the ability to be present with ourselves, our partners and the dance that arises. Guided explorations will increase our discernment and enjoyment of the experiences of momentum, pressure, support, weightbearing and movement. We will practise ‘not doing’ some of what arises automatically in order to access more possibility in our responses to stimuli and the creativity to be found with ease in the present. We will also learn to notice and support the flow of choices and co-incidences that allow us to enjoy and influence the dance. The workshop will include quiet and subtle work as well as energetic and playful movement. Each participant is invited to participate according to their own ability and energy. It will be suited to experienced dancers and those newer to the form. Lucia Walker I am based in Wales and work internationally as an Alexander teacher and Movement artist. I have been dancing all my life and involved with AT and Contact Improvisation for 40 years. I continue to practise, perform and teach Contact Improvisation and Instant Composition, exploring how to easily enter a creative state of presence. I also teach AT to groups and individuals as well as training teachers. Enjoyment of the sense of vitality and well-being that arose from learning Alexander Technique, and a desire to support people to be more fully themselves and to express themselves more easily in their chosen life activities, stimulated me to train as a teacher. I met Contact Improvisation in 1982, studying with Steve Paxton, Nancy Stark Smith, Kirstie Simson and practising through teaching with Jointwork Dance Group and others.Both AT and CI work with principles that enable us to consciously co-operate with our human design, able to be both active and receptive, calm and lively. I continue to practise, perform and teach Contact Improvisation and Instant Composition, exploring how to easily enter a creative state of presence. I facilitate regularly for Independent Dance (London), Forgotten Angle Theatre Collaborative (FATC) at Ebhudlweni rural arts centre South Africa) and participate in collaborative projects as well as continuing solo work.Lifelong learning is supported by teaching people from a wide range of ages, abilities and cultural backgrounds. I love the way movement exploration, awareness practices and play can provide both a method of self-care and a ‘common language’ which lies beneath different cultural experiences and styles of dance https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-shb2z-20260425110000

sessions.title: SKILLS FOR JAMMING CI Class with Caroline Waters& Soundscape by Stefano Ancora
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00 Contact Improvisation Class with Caroline Waters "Skills for Jamming" Referencing Steve Paxtons 'Material for the Spine", the class will focus on fundamentals of Ci that underpin the dancing which also draws from ki aikido. We will explore moving from a more relaxed and centred sense as we move in and out of contact with each other and the floor. Listening, sensing, following the flow, supporting and negotiating through touch the various exits and entrances to the dance. working in solo, duet and more, honing our listening and responding skills for dancing.. Caroline Waters is a Freelance Performer, Organiser and Arts Educator currently based in Brighton UK. Mainly drawing from CI and New Dance practices, She teaches, performs and directs in various situations ranging from the performic to the therapeutic, working with both professionals and non-professionals. The work often focuses on collaboration and working with other Art forms, which includes experience of working with a range of non-theatre spaces as a locus for performance. She taught Contextual Practice for 11 years at Dartington College or Arts and for the past 14 years was an associate Lecturer in CI at Chichester University UK. sessions.title: DYNAMIC CONNECTION Contact Improvisation WORKSHOP with Mark Rietema
sessions.details: Goldsmiths University - room 154, Main Reception- Richard Hoggart Building, Lewisham Way, London, UK, SE14 6NW • Dynamic Connection - Contact Improvisation Workshop Sat 9th May 11am-5pm For this day workshop we'll set the ground for a more explorative and dynamic dance with ourselves and with others. Early developmental movement patterns, movement puzzles and partner explorations will bring us into our dancing body, with a specific focus on differentiating and connecting our center and periphery. We’ll spend time with our fascia and the felt sense of our connective tissue, and how this shapes our dance and keeps us present in the unknown during lifts. Particularly I’d like to slow lifts down, so that we can override our fear responses from a place of groundedness, and explore the subtle shifts of tone and placement that offer new directions and possibilities. From this detailed and attentive place we’ll find our way back into a more embodied improvisational space through scores and practices where technique can soften, and where dancing feels grounded, safe and genuinely playful. The work draws on the gentle, listening qualities of Body-Mind Centering® as a foundation for more dynamic exploration, and is open to all levels. Mark Rietema teaches Somatics and CI in London and abroad, and offers workshops, lectures and group facilitation around embodiment, dance and mental health in places such as King’s College, the NHS and grassroots groups. He works as a psychotherapist and somatic movement educator, having studied Process Oriented Psychology and Body-Mind Centering®. His previous background is in community arts projects and performances (UK, US) with an MA in Community Arts from Goldsmiths. He teaches as faculty for Embody Move UK, Institut Prozessarbeit Deutschland, and as a guest teacher in various institutions. www.markrietema.com https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-sim4j-20260509110000

sessions.title: 'These Small Things' CI Class with Josephine Dyer & Soundscape by Ronen Kozokaro
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00. These Small Things This class explores some of the small moves that can form the fabric of a contact dance: scoops, tilts and see-saws that allow a tiny moment of uplift or sweep us gently off our feet. Starting with a solo warm-up of movement patterns and structured improvisations, we will move to partner tasks which encourage exploration and problem-solving. As we dance together these small exchanges can develop into bigger moves…or perhaps instead foster an appreciation of the ‘unspectacular’. Jo Dyer is a dancer and teacher, who has trained predominantly in release-based contemporary dance and improvisation. She has practised contact improvisation for many years and trained at Nancy Stark Smith’s January workshop in 2006. Her choreographic work includes A Tin Drum (Impulstanz Salzburg), Viva la Vida (Linbury Theatre) and Threefold (finalist at masdanza). Jo recently completed a MA in Creative Practice at TrinityLaban, with a Leverhulme Trust Dance Scholarship, where her final project investigated using contact improvisation to make politically engaged performance. sessions.title: 'ReSourcing your CI Practice: what moves you?' Workshop with Colleen Bartley
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • ReSourcing your CI Practice: what moves you? Working with foundational principles of CI this workshop explores Contact Improvisation as research into deep listening, tuning the senses and attention, emergent composition and self awareness. Over the course of the day we will explore quiet perceptual practices and more dynamic dancing, creating space for reflection, experimentation and embodied decision-making. Through touch, movement and attention, we will investigate how dances arise from changing relationships — between bodies, gravity, sensation, imagination and space. We'll explore how life moves through evolutionary patterns and work with materials from Nancy Stark Smith, Steve Paxton, Nita Little and other founders of CI with influences from Lisa Nelson, and Caryn McHose. The day will include solo, duet and group practices, scores, improvisational structures and time for reflection. We will explore responsiveness, composition in the moment, and ways of expanding perception through movement. We welcome curiosity, experimentation and different physical experiences. The class aims to cultivate an inclusive environment for curiosity, exploration, embodied research, critical reflection, dialogue and dancing together. Sources/Influences: Tuning Scores, Experiential Anatomy, The Underscore, Instant Composition, Neurological diversity, post-modern dance, Evolutionary Biology, BMC, Somatic Education, Relational Intelligence, Embryology, Functional Movement Colleen Bartley has a Dance and Education degree from Swarthmore College and a diploma in Community Dance from Laban with years of experience with Somatic Education. She co-organised London Contact Improvisation and was a co-editor at Contact Quarterly. She has worked directly with most of the founders of Contact Improvisation and been influenced by teachers of contemporary and post-modern dance, experiential anatomy with Gary Carter and Caryn McHose, Authentic Movement with Susan Schell, Improvisation & Composition (Tuning Scores with Lisa Nelson, The Underscore with Nancy Stark Smith, Ensemble Thinking with Nina Martin, Relational Intelligence with Nita Little) and is very active in dancing, organizing, writing about and documenting dance. Colleen has an invisible disability which informs her practice as a teacher and an artist. She believes that its everyone’s birthright to move, that people are inherently creative, movement is our first language, and sees dance as a way to empower people, to effect social change and to build community. sessions.title: THE ARC OF ATTENTION CI Class with Justin Philpott & Jam Soundscape by Carlo Natoli
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00. THE ARC OF ATTENTION This class explores circular movement as a pathway into contact improvisation. Beginning in stillness, we gently notice the journey of our awareness as we flow into self-led movement. From there, we explore circles, arcs and spirals through the body, allowing rolling, curving and looping pathways to emerge through improvisation. From this solo exploration, we gradually move into relation: first dancing with an awareness of others from a distance, then drawing closer, and eventually finding touch. We will then practise the skill of projecting from the ground, through the centre, and towards the point of contact with each other. With this experience in place, we return to circular movement and explore how contact becomes clearer: how we attend, meet, support and move with another through a shared point of connection. From there, the class opens naturally into the jam. About Justin: Justin is the host and resident teacher of the Brighton CI Community. He is a multidisciplinary mover with a background in martial arts and Thai-style bodywork. He has been dancing CI for over 12 years, teaching CI since 2018, and runs Moving Stillness - School of Somatics (https://www.moving-stillness.com). sessions.title: 'Unfolding Spirals' CI Class (1-3pm)with Simonetta Alessandri and Jam with sound by Stefano Ancora(3-6pm)
sessions.details: Rose Lipman Building, De Beauvoir Road, 43 De Beauvoir road, London, Uk, N1 5SF • Unfolding spirals In this class, we will explore spiral pathways moving from periphery to centre and back again, engaging the whole body in an efficient and organic way. Beginning with a short Feldenkrais exploration, we will tune into sensation, awareness, and the body’s internal intelligence. We will investigate how internal clarity supports listening and communication with a partner, helping us distribute weight with greater ease and deepen awareness of the relationship between the pelvis, limbs, and spine. Effortless lifts will emerge through gravity, momentum, and shared support, opening the infinite and exciting possibilities of spiralling. Simonetta is an Italian dance artist and somatic educator based in London, with more than 35 years of experience in the field. She is an internationally recognized teacher who has been practicing, performing, and teaching Contact Improvisation since 1991. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-sfa0j-20260614130000

sessions.title: Monday Contact Improv: Class + Jam
sessions.details: Rose Lipman Building, 43 De Beauvoir Road, London, N1 5SQ • Doors open at 6.20pm Please note they won't be an intro circle anymore. First timers can arrive a bit earlier for any questions and info around consent or about the jam. I also advise newcomers to familiarise themselves with the guidelines. This week we have a class taught by Marie, an experienced CI practitioner (22 years), professional dancer and choreographer. Theme and Content The theme of the class will be posted closer to the time in the CI Monday Jam WhatsApp group. You can join in following this link: https://chat.whatsapp.com/Iivbz8LzDvK5lGkiitQgcH (please note I will ask you a few question about yourself before I allow you into the chat, when you join please introduce yourself so we know who's in the chat) These Contact Improvisation classes give participants tools to be in touch and communicate through touch, and feel more confident joining a jam. There are open levels and benefit beginners, improvers and experienced practitioners. There is a mix of somatic guided exploration to be embodied, usually in solo, some mindful and focused explorations with one or several partners, and some more technical tools or short patterns. This will be followed by a 45min-1h jam Jam The emphasis of the jam is to explore movement while being in physical contact with other movers. Participants are encouraged to experience it as an embodied research and a practice where everyone can bring their own research, interest and physicality into the space. Jamming is the best way to try out, take risks, learn from each other, observe, connect, be surprised, practice setting boundaries, dance and play with people we may not know or expect to dance with. Participants are asked to take responsibility for themselves. Levels and abilities: The space welcomes professional dancers, amateurs, seasoned contact improvisers and beginners, people from all backgrounds and abilities. It requires every participants to take responsibility for their own bodies, so if you need extra support or a carer or someone to look after you then you can bring them for free. Note for beginners: the jam is not guided. The facilitator is there to hold the space but doesn't guide the jam. What is Contact Improvisation (CI)? Contact Improvisation is movement research that explores the physical principles of touch, momentum, and shared weight in relation to gravity. It involves a constant dialogue between two or more movers who respond to each other’s movements in real time, often maintaining physical contact but also playing with proximity and space. This practice emphasises spontaneity, trust, and mutual awareness, allowing dancers to create unique movement for each encounter. Event page: https://millco.co.uk/events/contact-improvisation-jam/ https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-su4pn-20260420183000

sessions.title: Monday Contact Improv Jam
sessions.details: Rose Lipman Building, 43 De Beauvoir Road, London, N1 • Doors open at 6.20pm Please note they won't be an intro circle anymore. First timers can arrive a bit earlier for any questions and info around consent or about the jam. I also advise newcomers to familiarise themselves with the guidelines. This weekly jam invites movers to jam together for a regular practice. Warming-up: Marie Chabert will facilitate the warming-up before the jam. Contact Improvisation is movement research that explores the physical principles of touch, momentum, and shared weight in relation to gravity. It involves a constant dialogue between two or more movers who respond to each other’s movements in real time, often maintaining physical contact but also playing with proximity and space. sessions.title: **Women’s Touch : Community as a relational process with Marie Chabert & Manuela Albrecht**
sessions.details: Siobhan Davies Studios, 85 Saint George's Road, London, SE1 6ER • Embodiment - Community - Safe Space Develop self-enquiry and autonomy- Be in touch - Share impressions + experiences + food For this edition Marie Chabert is inviting the dance practitioner Manuela Albrecht to share her beautiful research on mycelium and inter-connectivity. Manuela and Marie will facilitate this Contact Impro workshop/laboratory exploring how community forms through relation and difference. Weaving between movement scores and moments of shared reflection, participants are invited to experience connection in and out of contact using yarns, a soft fibrous material used for crochet. We will open up questions and investigations such as: -How do I enter and inhabit a group? -What helps me feel connected without erasing difference? -Is community something we belong to, or something we continuously make? Work unfolds through solo and group scores, supporting attentive connection, experimentation, and self-enquiry. The space intends to be safe, consent-based, and accessible to all. No prior experience with Contact Improvisation is required. Rough schedule (may change slightly) 11.30am-12pm – Welcome tea & chat (meeting each other + time & space to land) 12pm –1.30pm – Intro to Consent, solo warm-up and collective embodied explorations 1.30-2.30pm – Lunchtime: food sharing 2.30-5.30pm – Group score playing with the yarns. Small group reflection: community as a relational process Witnessing, writing, sharing and integrating Who is this for? Those who identify as women and non-binary interested in bringing more awareness to physical interactions in dancing and in Contact Improvisation It welcomes all levels and can be applied to other practices than Contact Improvisation. In the past people were able to apply it to dance therapy, theatre, psychology... What is Women's Touch? Women’s touch is an event curated by Marie Chabert, that offers a space for those who identify as women and non-binary to explore movement practices that are mainly based on touch (Contact Improvisation, somatic practices, improvisation) in a safe environment. It aims to empower women to claim their space and redefine it within a framework that challenges prevailing patriarchal norms. It offers both a place to embody and share our experiences verbally. The intention is to foster a supportive environment, where women and non-binary participants feel safe to take risks, be vulnerable, and share their humanity, free from the pressure of dominant dynamics. Cultivating such spaces requires trust, shared language, and mutual care. It allows us not just to survive, but to thrive in connection. Manuela's bio: I’m a Brazilian interdisciplinary movement artist, facilitator, performer, and PhD researcher working at the intersection of dance, theatre, community, and micro-politics. My practice explores movement as a way to reflect on the urgencies of our time. I’m especially drawn to collaborative processes and to disrupt colonial legacies, the normative, and stay radically tender, embodied, and real. https://manualbrecht5.wixsite.com/manualbrecht/ If you have financial difficulties and want to take part, please get in touch. photo by Sol Ji Park https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-sv4ry-20260510113000

It involves a constant dialogue between two or more movers who respond to each other’s movements in real time, often maintaining physical contact but also playing with proximity and space. This practice emphasises spontaneity, trust, and mutual awareness, allowing dancers to create unique movement for each encounter. Event page: https://millco.co.uk/events/contact-improvisation-jam/ https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-sqjez-20260706183000

sessions.title: **Women’s Touch : Community as a relational process - workshop lab**
sessions.details: Siobhan Davies Studios, 85 Saint George's Road, London, SE1 6ER • Embodiment - Community - Develop self-enquiry and agency- Be in touch - Share reflection + Experiences + Food After the great success and feedback from the last Women's Touch event, I've decided to put together another one to follow-up on this inspiring and rich theme of Community as a relational process. What's different from the last one? More lab time, more time to co-create, journal and share impressions. We won't use yarns this time around but a different material... I'll reveal it later. What is it? Marie will facilitate this Contact Impro workshop/laboratory exploring how community forms through relation and difference. Weaving between movement scores and moments of shared reflection, participants are invited to experience connection in and out of contact. We will open up questions and investigations such as: -How do I enter and inhabit a group? -What helps me feel connected without erasing difference? -Is community something we belong to, or something we continuously make? Work unfolds through solo and group scores, supporting attentive connection, experimentation, and self-enquiry. The space intends to be safe, consent-based, and accessible to all. Rough schedule (may change slightly) 11.30am-12pm – Welcome tea & chat (meeting each other + time & space to land) 12pm –1.30pm – Intro to Consent, solo warm-up and collective embodied explorations 1.30-2.30pm – Lunchtime: food sharing 2.30-5.30pm – Group scores Small group reflection: community as a relational process Witnessing, writing, sharing and integrating Who is this for? Those who identify as women and non-binary interested in bringing more awareness to physical interactions in dancing and in Contact Improvisation It welcomes all levels and can be applied to other practices than Contact Improvisation. In the past people were able to apply it to dance therapy, theatre, psychology... This lab is open level. No prior experience of Contact Improv is required What is Women's Touch? Women’s touch is an event curated by Marie Chabert, that offers a space for those who identify as women and non-binary to explore movement practices that are mainly based on touch (Contact Improvisation, somatic practices, improvisation) in a safe environment. It aims to empower women to claim their space and redefine it within a framework that challenges prevailing patriarchal norms. It offers both a place to embody and share our experiences verbally. The intention is to foster a supportive environment, where women and non-binary participants feel safe to take risks, be vulnerable, and share their humanity, free from the pressure of dominant dynamics. Cultivating such spaces requires trust, shared language, and mutual care. It allows us not just to survive, but to thrive in connection. If you have financial difficulties and want to take part, please get in touch. photo by Sol Ji Park https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-sq6op-20260712113000

sessions.title: Monday Contact Improv Jam : dress-up jam @centre 151
sessions.details: Centre 151, 151 Whiston Road, London, E2 8GU • Doors open at 6.20pm Please note they won't be an intro circle anymore. First timers can arrive a bit earlier for any questions and info around consent or about the jam. I also advise newcomers to familiarise themselves with the guidelines. This weekly jam invites movers to jam together for a regular practice. Warming-up: Marie Chabert will facilitate the warming-up before the jam. The aim is for participants to arrive into their body, tune in, connect to themselves, to others and to the space around. It brings people into their senses and embodied awareness. It is not a class and doesn't give the same technical tools and principles as a class. Classes are held every other week and will give you these skills. Jam The emphasis of the jam is to explore movement while being in physical contact with other movers. Participants are encouraged to experience it as an embodied research and a practice where everyone can bring their own research, interest and physicality into the space. Jamming is the best way to try out, take risks, learn from each other, observe, connect, be surprised, practice setting boundaries, dance and play with people we may not know or expect to dance with. Participants are asked to take responsibility for themselves. Levels and abilities: The space welcomes professional dancers, amateurs, seasoned contact improvisers and beginners who took a few classes beforehand, people from all backgrounds and abilities. It requires every participants to take responsibility for their own bodies, so if you need extra support or a carer or someone to look after you then you can bring them for free. Note for beginners: the jam is not guided. The facilitator is there to hold the space but doesn't guide the jam. What is Contact Improvisation (CI)? Contact Improvisation is movement research that explores the physical principles of touch, momentum, and shared weight in relation to gravity. It involves a constant dialogue between two or more movers who respond to each other’s movements in real time, often maintaining physical contact but also playing with proximity and space. This practice emphasises spontaneity, trust, and mutual awareness, allowing dancers to create unique movement for each encounter. Connect to the community If you want to join the CI Monday Jam WhatsApp group, click on this link: https://chat.whatsapp.com/Iivbz8LzDvK5lGkiitQgcH (when you join please introduce yourself so we know who's in the chat) More events on mariechabert.com https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-s47od-20260713183000

sessions.title: **Women’s Touch with special guest: Dr Vanio Papadelli**
sessions.details: Siobhan Davies Studios, 85 Saint George's Road, London, SE1 6ER • Embodiment - Community - Develop self-enquiry and agency- Be in touch - Share reflection + Experiences + Food Who is this for? Those who identify as women and non-binary interested in bringing more awareness to physical interactions in dancing and in Contact Improvisation It welcomes all levels and can be applied to other practices than Contact Improvisation. In the past people were able to apply it to dance therapy, theatre, psychology... This lab is open level. No prior experience of Contact Improv is required What is Women's Touch? Women’s touch is an event curated by Marie Chabert, that offers a space for those who identify as women and non-binary to explore movement practices that are mainly based on touch (Contact Improvisation, somatic practices, improvisation) in a safe environment. It aims to empower women to claim their space and redefine it within a framework that challenges prevailing patriarchal norms. It offers both a place to embody and share our experiences verbally. The intention is to foster a supportive environment, where women and non-binary participants feel safe to take risks, be vulnerable, and share their humanity, free from the pressure of dominant dynamics. Cultivating such spaces requires trust, shared language, and mutual care. It allows us not just to survive, but to thrive in connection. About Vanio Papadelli I am an interdisciplinary performance artist and educator based in London. I teach across HE Performing Arts Institutions (mainly Rose Bruford College and Trinity Laban Conservatoire of Music and Dance) and as a freelancer. With movement and the senses at the core of my practice, I interact with other media including text, video, everyday objects and natural materials to create intimate, multi-layered, poetic and surreal environments as spaces of connection, reminiscence and change. My artistic practice explores raw and tender territories of vulnerability, memory, care and candidness in human relationships imbued in more-than-human worlds. I have dealt especially with mother-daughter relationships, female friendships, experiences of giving birth and mothering. My work is based in collaborative and interdisciplinary processes and has travelled in Greece, UK, Italy, Poland, Germany, Egypt and Lebanon. https://vaniopapadelli.com/ If you have financial difficulties and want to take part, please get in touch. photo by Sol Ji Park https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-s61bh-20261122110000

sessions.title: Moving Encounters - An experiential workshop exploring embodied relational awareness for therapists and practitioners
sessions.details: Centre 151, 151 Whiston Road, London, E2 8GU • About the workshop A one-day experiential workshop exploring embodied awareness and relational presence through structured movement-based inquiry. This is a guided and contained space for working with attention, sensation, movement, and relation as they arise in real time. The emphasis is on direct experience rather than interpretation, performance, or technique. The day is structured, but not prescriptive, allowing space for both individual exploration and relational processes with others. The atmosphere of the day The workshop provides a structured experiential setting for exploring how awareness is organised in the body and in relation. At times the work will be internally focused and quiet. At other times it will involve movement in relation to others, with clear frameworks supporting consent, choice, and pacing throughout. Throughout the day, the intention is to stay close to what is actually happening in experience, moment by moment. Approaches informing the work This workshop draws on principles from: Body-Mind CenteringⓇ — somatic and developmental awareness of the body Authentic Movement — witnessed movement and reflective awareness Contact Improvisation — weight, responsiveness, and relational movement These are not taught as techniques, but used as reference points within structured experiential inquiry. What will be explored Across the day, the work will include: -attention to sensation, impulse, and movement -how awareness shifts between self and other -relational contact and distance -witnessing and being witnessed -patterns of responsiveness in interaction -how experience is received and registered in the body Participation is always invitational, and there is space to observe or pause at any time. Who this is for This workshop is open to therapists, counsellors, psychotherapists, and practitioners working in relational or embodied contexts. It is designed for those interested in exploring embodied awareness as part of their reflective practice, particularly those curious about how bodily experience shapes presence, attention, and relational responsiveness. No previous movement or dance experience is required. What this is not This is not: a dance or performance workshop a training in any therapeutic modality a group therapy or personal process group a space for psychological interpretation or treatment It is an experiential workshop for embodied inquiry and relational exploration. Reflection and integration There will be structured opportunities to reflect verbally on experience during the day. Reflection is offered as a way of noticing and articulating experience, rather than interpreting or analysing it. Participants are encouraged to consider how the work may relate to their own professional practice. About the facilitator This workshop is facilitated by Mark Rietema, a UKCP-registered Psychotherapist, trained in Process Work, and Somatic Movement Therapist (BMCA) with a background spanning clinical practice, somatic education, and movement facilitation. Alongside a clinical practice that has included extensive work within NHS mental health services, Mark teaches and facilitates in a range of professional and academic contexts, including Embody-Move UK, Institut Prozessarbeit Deutschland, and King's College London, where they contributed to research on arts and wellbeing in higher education. He teaches Body-Mind Centering within official BMCⓇ trainings in the UK, Spain, and Italy. Mark also teaches Contact Improvisation nationally and internationally, and is part of the organising team of the London Contact Festival and CI@Goldsmiths. This direct grounding in CI practice informs the relational and movement dimensions of the workshop. A background in community arts (MA, Goldsmiths) and experience facilitating internationally - including in the USA, Palestine, and Germany - continues to shape an approach that is both rigorous and attentive to the lived complexity of group work. Mark will be supported throughout the day by Sole de la Hoz, a Contact Improvisation teacher and trainee psychotherapist in Process Oriented Psychology, with a professional background in dance. Cancellation policy - full refund until 24th of May, 50% refund until 6th June, after that contact us to see if we can offer a transfer for a future workshop. https://bookwhen.com/markrietema/e/ev-s2y7g-20260621100000

sessions.title: Breath - Space - Volume CI Workshop - Zürich (13-18 Uhr)
sessions.details: Nistplatz, Limmatalstrasse 215, 8049 Zürich (Höngg), 8049 • Contact Improvisation workshop taught by Sole and Mark A day of somatic explorations, technical skills and improvisational work Breath as a gateway and bridge between sensing and moving from our inner landscape, the wider space and others. Our breath can offer us easy access to modulate our tone and arrive in a sensory and dancing body. It shapes, shifts and influences all parts of our bodies, and it adds timing, rhythms and focus in relation to others. As we expand or condense our volume, we find new possibilities for weight-sharing, momentum and contact. We’ll move between gentle somatic explorations, more specific experiential anatomy of our lungs based on Body-Mind CenteringⓇ, and choreographic and technical CI aspects. Exploring how we can connect our breaths to that of others, and use the volume of our bodies to under- and overdance. You can expect a mixture of solo explorations, technical skills exercises and open group scores that emphasise the improvisational nature of CI. Open to all levels. Mark Rietema (DE/UK) Mark teaches Somatics and CI in London and abroad, and offers workshops, lectures and group facilitation around embodiment, dance and mental health in Universities, the NHS and grassroots groups. He works as a psychotherapist and somatic educator, having studied Process Oriented Psychology and Body-Mind Centering®. His previous background is in community art projects and performances (UK, US) with an MA in Community Arts (Goldsmiths). He teaches as faculty for Embody Move UK and the Institut Prozessarbeit Deutschland, and as an associate in Kings College. Mark is part of the organising team for the London Contact Festival and Goldsmiths jams & classes. www.markrietema.com Soledad de la Hoz (ES/UK) Sole trained at the Rambert School of Ballet and Contemporary Dance. She has performed extensively in opera and theatre productions (ROH, Royal Court Theatre, Glyndebourne, ENO) as well as immersive dance and screen productions, working with notable choreographers such as Aleta Collins, Anna Morrissey, Maxine Doyle, Marie-Gabrielle Rotie, Scarlett Mackmin, Jennifer White and Lisa Welham. In addition to her professional dancing, Sole is a trained Thai massage therapist and has a deep love and interest in Contact. Improvisation has an immense positive benefit on her movement practice and well-being. She is very happy to continue developing in this field by training and teaching. Photo: Patrick Beelaert https://bookwhen.com/markrietema/e/ev-s1cq1-20260912000000

sessions.title: "SENSATIONAL MOVES": 9 -12 April . 4 DAY INTENSIVE Feldenkrais, Movement Exploration & CI with Simonetta Alessandri & Thomas Kampe
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • 9 -12 April 4 day Intensive exploring somatic dance practice, Feldenkrais Method and Contact Improvisation. In this four-day workshop, we combine somatic movement principles of the Feldenkrais Method® with dance exploration & Contact Improvisation practice. In the morning sessions we will be using ‘Awareness Through Movement’® lessons and touch-based explorations. We will focus on solo explorations and will connect with our core and imagination, deepening our felt sense of gravity and grounding. Through Dance Improvisational practice we will develop our observation skills and individual creative agency to integrate our embodied discoveries, fostering creative risk-taking and playfulness. In the afternoon sessions we will expand these explorations into the practice of Contact Improvisation to enhance our ability to interact and communicate expressively and playfully with ease and clarity. Drawing on our felt sense and somatic attunement through touch and gravity, we will revisit and refine Contact Improvisation skills and creative vocabularies, such as weight exchange, balance, support and lifts with a partner and in small ensemble situations. Each day we will take time to discuss or write reflectively about our personal and somatic discoveries. The Feldenkrais Method®, developed by Dr. Moshe Feldenkrais (1904-1984) is a gentle, embodied process focused on self-discovery, embodied thinking, creative wellbeing, and functional improvement. It invites participants to slow down, listen to internal sensations, and create new neural pathways that support creative choice making in action. This workshop is open to anyone interested in deepening their dance or movement practice. Thomas and Simonetta have been teaching Feldenkrais-informed dance classes around the globe for over 20 years and are world-leading researchers and artists in the field. Dr.Thomas Kampe (PhD) (he/him) has worked as a performer, artist, researcher and somatic educator across the globe for 40 years and was ‘Professor of Somatic Performance and Education’ at Bath Spa University (UK) between 2012 and 2022. Thomas initially trained in visual art and dance and is a teacher of the Feldenkrais Method ® which forms a rich foundation for his teaching, arts practice and research. He is interested in critical somatic arts legacies and his writings and performance works have been published internationally. He was editor of the Feldenkrais Research Journal Vol. 6 ‘Practices of Freedom: The Feldenkrais Method & Creativity’ in 2019, and recently co-edited JDSP Vol. 13.1 & 2 (2022): ‘Embodying Eco-Consciousness: Somatics, Aesthetic Practice and Social Action’. Simonetta Alessandri is an Italian dance artist & somatic educator based in London. She applies the Feldenkrais Method in dance & movement training and in performance making. She teaches at Trinity Laban, Goldsmiths University and London Contemporary Dance School. Her work is informed by more than 30 years of dancing, teaching and choreographing. Her choreography has been for dance companies, student pieces, large scale opera, improvised performance, site specific and movement direction for theatre. She was one of the few dance practitioners in Italy who began working with CI in the 90’s. She has been a guest teacher in Germany, Colombia, UK, Norway, Israel, France, Switzerland, Italy, Poland, Spain and Taiwan. She obtained the Post Graduate Diploma at London Contemporary Dance School; she is a qualified teacher of the Feldenkrais Method and she holds the Teacher Certificate of the Royal Academy of Dance. She is a founder member of CI@Goldsmiths London. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-swh5j-20260409100000

sessions.title: Weaving Spatial Support into the Body, Laura Doehler
sessions.details: Rockingham Community Centre, Falmouth Rd, London SE1 6PD, UK • In this workshop we will explore how our weight always is supported by default and acts as support simply through being in space and sensing our own physical space. We strip back expectations around support to uncover how a changing physical landscape of body and space create a container that at times carries us through space and at other times means we become a carrier or both. Our intention is to challenge our ideas around support so we won't get locked into carrying or loading and acquire greater skills in falling through letting go and expansion. We will probe spatial anchoring by tapping into the expansive qualities that space offers, find lightness and the ability to redirect oneself. These processes shall support a greater freedom in our dancing inside and outside the studio as we reckon with our ideas and expectations around support.

sessions.title: Introduction to CI
sessions.details:
Walworth Living Room, All Saints Hall, Surrey Square, London SE17 2JU, UK • https://www.eventbrite.com/e/1977172768976?aff=oddtdtcreator

In this gentle workshop, we’ll offer simple, playful ways to connect through movement. Contact Improvisation, also called CI, can include touch, sharing weight, or dynamic motion, but we will keep everything light, optional, and based on comfort.

We want this event to be accessible to all abilities, ages and backgrounds.

We will offer ways to mobilise your body to begin with which everyone adapts as needed. It is important that you choose how you want to move, and who you want to move with.

sessions.title: CI Upside Down Lewis Wilkins and Laura Doehler
sessions.details:
Rockingham Community Centre, Falmouth Rd, London SE1 6QP, UK • https://www.eventbrite.com/e/1989991974565?aff=oddtdtcreator

In this workshop, we will explore how inversions in contact can open up your range and play in contact improvisation. Overall it is a workshop for experienced dancers who are looking to become more comfortable in their upside down practice and who like to increase risk in their dancing safely. That means we will also look at falls and jumps to incorporate more dynamic weight shifts. We will take time to prepare the body and develop a deeper understanding of recovery, safety, and freedom in movement as we go along for a strong yet mindful practice to emerge.

sessions.title: London CI Weekender - 2-days of Contact Improvisation, Exploring the concepts of 'reaching and meeting' and 'elements of the jam' w/ Josh Jones and Lily Antonia
sessions.details:
Willesden, London, UK •

London CI Weekender
A 2-day Contact Improvisation Workshop

https://dandelion.events/e/londonciweekender

Whats on offer :

A weekend of movement, exploration, co-collaboration and connection. Diving into the fundamentals & philosophy of Contact Improvisation. The London CI weekender offers space and guidance to engage with the practice, tune into the body & develop the skills that serve us both in the dance and in daily life. Bringing shared inquiry to the foreground to drop deeper through the layers of what is possible through CI.

Each day has a distinct focus, and participants are welcome to attend either day or the full weekend.

Come deepen your connection to the ground, space, and others while gaining tools for (contact) improvisation, jamming, and attuned awareness through the joy of movement.

Saturday: Reaching & Meeting

How can we meet the earth, meet ourselves and meet another?

How is it to be met?

Investigating and understanding mutual meeting as a core part of the dance.

Reaching as a tool to transform into oscillating expansive movers. To expand awareness and expand the ability of the body. Coming away from collapse and into lightness. Effortlessly working with gravity and momentum.

The day will include technical insights and the approach is practice as research. Allowing ourselves to be informed by the dance - discover how to move functionally and fluidly, open to all that's available in the present. Through solo, partner & group exercises we’ll explore reaching and meeting as a shared point of attention.

This day offers a grounding in presence, deep listening, and mutual direction.

Sunday: Elements of the Jam


On Sunday, we move into the world of the jam. We’ll explore how to enter and exit dances, navigate shared space, and respond to each unfolding moment. Through structured improvisation scores and instant compositions, we’ll build tools for the jam, develop adaptability, and co-create through group improvisation.

A container for emergence and spontaneity.

Meet your facilitators

Lily Antonia (She/Her)

Dance & Movement Artist | Facilitator

Lily is a contemporary dance artist, specialising in contact improvisation. She has been performing, teaching and choreographing internationally since graduating from Trinity Laban Conservatoire of Music and Dance. Working with companies Wired Aerial Theatre, Mad Dogs Dance Theatre, and Company Chameleon. Lily is co-director of contemporary dance and live art company UMAMI-i!-MOVEMENT and co-founder of Contact Improv MCR.

Her approach to CI blends playful exploration with technical clarity. Inviting curiosity, challenging potential and nurturing individual creativity - Lily invites dancers to bring an aliveness to the present. Creating spaces where dancers can listen, trust, and move with intention and spontaneity.

“To cultivate a state of being - in readiness without anticipation... we open ourselves to

all possibilities"

Joshua Jarvis Jones (He/They)

Dancer | Facilitator | Choreographer

Josh is a contemporary dancer and facilitator whose practice is rooted in emotional exploration and embodied connection. Following a time of personal loss, he found dance—especially contact improvisation—as a vital tool for healing, grounding, and rediscovering relationships with self and others.

Trained at the Northern School of Contemporary Dance, Josh has performed internationally with artists like Ricardo Buscarini and Madeline Shan, exploring themes of intimacy and identity. His deep engagement with contact improvisation bridges technique and relational psychology, and he shares this through workshops, labs, and festivals worldwide.

Josh co founded *Lean_In*, a nomadic movement series that has grown into the annual retreat *Lean_In_Nature*, inviting people into co-creative, embodied research. He has taught at CI hubs and circus events across Europe. His work invites participants into a dance of authenticity, where movement becomes a meeting place for truth and transformation.

INCLUSIVE AND SAFE SPACE

We are committed to creating an inclusive and safe space for all participants. This workshop is designed to be accessible to all and participants are welcome to let us know in advance about specific access needs.

Who’s the workshop for

Open to all levels. Whether you’re a seasoned pro or you've never done the practice before there is something for everyone. No previous dance experience required just a willingness to explore & create.

Where

Location will be given upon booking.

We will be dancing in the heart of Camberwell, the space has a kitchen available for use by participants, toilets and showers. Access to the hall is via a flight of stairs.

If you would like to attend but have financial concerns / are unable to purchase a ticket due to financial reasons please reach out - we will find the way with you.

This event is 18+

If you have any queries / questions / thoughts or just want to say hi please feel free to get in touch - leaninmovement123@gmail.com

https://dandelion.events/e/londonciweekender

LONDON JAM
sessions.details:
https://www.eventbrite.com/e/london-jam-tickets-1990466643312?aff=oddtdtcreator&keep_tld=true

A CI jam to bring everyone together: London's CI teachers, organisers and you, the wider community for a time out to dance, lab and connect.


26th: Saturday 16:00 - 22:00

27th: Sunday 11:00-17:00


Our teaching and organising community is the foundation of London's CI community.
We want to celebrate being here together and meet you as a community, not just as individual practitioners.

Join us for one or two days of dancing, where you can fully let go—fall, fly, and be caught by the fabric of long-term, lush CI practices that our community holds. Experience CI as life integration where body meets mind and generates a collective field of care, curiosity and spirit.

We enter with as little agenda as possible, honouring the present moment that improvisation and jamming invite. Each day will be centred around spacious jams in the large Walworth Hall. Adjacent areas—the kitchen, living room, chill-out space and courtyard—give us room to chat, eat, rest, dream, and flow.

As our dancing and teaching community grows in size, practices, wisdom, and challenges, this event offers us a chance to jam our way into sensing who we are now and how we step into the future together.

Before and during the event, you are invited to share any questions you have about CI—technical, visionary, reflective, or concerning needs or serious concerns. We will dedicate 1.5 hours each day for teachers to explore these questions in small, spontaneous labs that you’re welcome to join on the spot.

And that’s really all there is to it!

This event is for people who consider CI an ongoing practice and who want to connect with the wider community through shared research and continuous exploration.

Please bring your own food. A microwave, cups, tea, and basic kitchen essentials are available to support your day.

sessions.title: Professional Class
sessions.details: *Please note, professional class now runs from Tuesday to Thursday (8:00am-9:30am). Our daily classes for professional-level dancers are led each week by a different artist from the international dance community. Each teacher brings their own style and technique to class, from their experience performing with world-leading dance companies and choreographers. With a strong focus on honing and maintaining technique, you’ll be challenged and inspired amongst peers. Professional class is ‘drop in’ and be booked by the day. You can still drop in and pay at Rambert, however as our spaces are strictly limited we recommend you book online before travelling to the building. Summer 2026 line up to be announced soon! Terms and Conditions of Booking If you need to cancel your booking, you can do so up to 48 hours before a drop in for a full refund in class credits by logging into your Momence account. If you'd prefer a monetary refund, just email us to let us know.

sessions.title: Contemporary for Over 60s
sessions.details: In this Contemporary class, you’ll be developing your contemporary technique while also learning some new moves – taking inspiration from a variety of traditional Caribbean dances. You will be able to create and play with those moves in a variety of ways. This class features a live musician. Led by Rambert faculty member Paradigmz. He’s worked alongside choreographers across contemporary and classical African Peoples Dance, as well Jazz and Hip Hop and Modern Dance Forms and offers extensive experience. Artists and companies include Sakoba Dance Theatre, Jackie Guy, (Norman Rubber) Stephenson and many more. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. There is on-street parking for Blue Badge holders with step-free access to the building, and lifts to all levels. Changing facilities are available on the first floor. A water fountain is available on the second floor- please bring your own reuse-able bottle for this. You can wear whatever your are comfortable moving in, and you’re welcome to wear dance shoes if you have them (although no dark/black soles) or bare-feet. Please make sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. Terms and Conditions of Booking If you need to cancel your booking, you can do so up to 48 hours before a drop in and up to 48 hours before the first class of a term booking for a full refund in class credits by logging into your Momence account. If you'd prefer a monetary refund, just email us to let us know.

sessions.title: Yoga Open Level
sessions.details: These classes focus on the fine details of yoga postures and breathing, working on both your body and mind. Generally, our yoga classes start with meditation and pranayama (manipulation of the breath) and warm-up postures, followed by standing and seated asanas, and a relaxing savasana to finish. Teachers of this class come from a variety of backgrounds and will have their own individual way of teaching. Open to all levels from complete beginners to the more experienced, as the teacher adapts the class according to the students. This class is for 18+. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. sessions.details: Hold ‘em like they do in Texas with our new line dance class. Learn a range of short, up tempo line dance routines to a range of music including Country & Western classics from the likes of Dolly Parton and Shania Twain, to current TikTok trends like Boots on the Ground and House of Bamboo. Heel tapping, thigh slapping and suitable for all experience and fitness levels, this class is all the Thursday evening fun you need to get ready for the weekend. Wear whatever is comfortable but to save our lovely dance floors, please wear jazz shoes or light soled trainers and leave your cowboy boots at the door. sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. sessions.title: Ballet Improvers
sessions.details: Nina’s upbeat class will explore placement, spatial expansion, lyricism, and musicality. The focus will be on artistic expression, and how correct technique and coordination facilitates pace and speed. Nina began as an actress and dancer, appearing in over 60 TV, film, and stage productions before moving on to teach and coach ballet companies and dancers. Suitable if: you are 18+ and comfortable with the basics of Ballet and want to progress your technique with new challenges and combinations. We'd recommend around one year of ballet experience for this class. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. sessions.title: Contemporary Intermediate
sessions.details: In this class, Belinda will guide you through a series of exercises which blend the use of torso, fall and recovery, spatial pathways, flow and momentum. Drawing from her training in Cunningham, Limon and Release techniques, class will start with short technical exercises that build into more complex travelling steps, dynamic swings and longer choreographic phrases. In this class a sense of community and shared experience is created, providing an environment where participants feel safe, challenge themselves and above all, have fun. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve taken a few months of contemporary dance classes before, are comfortable with the basics, and would like to develop your technique and creativity. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. sessions.title: The Playground
sessions.details: The Playground is an open creative space for Artists across the arts to come together to explore, collaborate and create in a supportive, welcoming environment. For dancers, sessions are aimed at those in their final years of professional training and those already working professionally. Artists from other creative disciplines are warmly welcome to join, create and collaborate. Whether you're looking to move, make, experiment or simply reconnect with your creativity, The Playground is a space to play, connect and be inspired.

sessions.title: ProDance class with Justin De Jager & Tia Hockey
sessions.details: In this class Justin and Tia will share elements of their current research of a hybrid movement approach, exploring an instinctive fusion of threading and contact improvisation/partnering. Threading’s precise, spatial geometry and entangling puzzles are integrated with contact improvisation’s weight-sharing dialogue and tactile negotiation. To begin, we will establish a shared understanding of chosen threading concepts through physicalised tools and concept-based tasks. This exploration will lead into the partnering aspect of the language, building our kinaesthetic awareness, activating embodied problem-solving skills and encouraging creative freedom through a guided improvisational practice. Photo: Jack Thomson

sessions.title: Gaga People
sessions.details: Gaga is the movement language developed by Ohad Naharin throughout many years, parallel to his work as a choreographer and artistic director (1990-2018) of Batsheva Dance Company. Gaga originated from Naharin’s need to communicate with his dancers and his curiosity in the ongoing research of movement. Gaga classes are predicated on a deep listening to the body and to physical sensations. The instructions are deployed to increase awareness of and further amplify sensation, and rather than turning from one prompt to another, information is layered, building into a multisensory, physically challenging experience. While many instructions are imbued with rich imagery, the research of Gaga is fundamentally physical, insisting on a specific process of embodiment. Inside this shared research, the improvisational nature of the exploration enables each participant’s deeply personal connection with Gaga. Throughout the class, participants are guided by a series of evocative instructions deployed to increase awareness of and further amplify sensation. Rather than turning from one prompt to another, information is layered, building into a multisensory, physically challenging experience. While many instructions are imbued with rich imagery, the research of Gaga is fundamentally physical, insisting on a specific process of embodiment. Inside this shared research, the improvisational nature of the exploration enables each participant’s deeply personal connection with Gaga. Participants should wear comfortable clothes and be prepared to dance barefoot or in socks. It is advisable to bring a bottle of water and a towel for use after class.

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Kieran Brown
sessions.details: 'A release-based class cultivating awareness through movement. The class aims to develop softness and somatic presence through form, playing with weight and flow to expand and inhabit our dancing bodies through space. We begin on the floor, guided by principles of Feldenkrais to build somatic awareness and awaken the body’s intelligence. Through gentle, intentional exploration of the skeletal structure, attuning to sensation, gradually building heat and structure as we make our way to standing. From this grounded foundation, we transition into improvised crossings, activating the senses through touch. We explore the body’s relationship to gravity—playing with the weight of the limbs, head, and pelvis, and riding the architecture of the body from within. As the class progresses, we learn set material, approaching it with an improvisational sensibility. Inviting this into choreographed phrases that explore soft expansion and length, playing with weight transference and coordination through space.’

sessions.title: YIN  WITH MYOFASCIAL RELEASE
sessions.details: Yin Myofascial Release (Yin MFR) is a slow, nourishing practice that combines long-held Yin poses with massage balls to gently melt away deep tension in the body. We’ll settle into passive stretches for a few minutes at a time, using props to apply steady, mindful pressure to areas like the hips, back, shoulders and feet. This helps release tight fascia, improve mobility, ease soreness and leave you feeling more spacious and hydrated from the inside out. It’s not about pushing, it’s about softening. As the body unwinds, the nervous system shifts into “rest and digest” mode, helping you feel grounded, calm and deeply relaxed. Suitable for most levels, this class is a beautiful way to care for your body, move more freely, and give yourself the reset you didn’t know you needed.

sessions.title: YANG TO YIN
sessions.details: This class will flow through the energies of Yang into Yin. The first half will be focused on dynamic vinyasa-style asana to connect with and work the physical body through strong postures, an awareness of breath, a deep connection to sensation and body-mindfulness. The second half is designed to stimulate the parasympathetic nervous system with long floor-based postures, supported by meditation and mindfulness techniques to calm, ground and reset.

sessions.title: ProDance class with Peter Butler
sessions.details: A physical class consisting of a full body warm up and guided improvisation tasks followed by a short phrase incorporating the elements explored at the start of class into choreography. The aim of the class is to feel connected to your full body. Encouraging curiosity in how your breath, weight and emotion affect your physicality.

sessions.title: Moving Together: Improvisation, Connection & Expression
sessions.details: In this class, James guides participants through improvisation practices that encourage honesty, curiosity, and personal expression through movement. Together, we will explore how to connect more deeply with ourselves, our bodies, and each other in a supportive and open environment. Through guided tasks and shared exploration, the class invites participants to discover joy in both dancing and witnessing others dance, while building a sense of community and connection. Rooted in playfulness and embodied practice, the sessions offer space to move freely, listen closely, and expand awareness through improvisation. Image by Yu-Ning Tan.

sessions.title: YANG TO YIN
sessions.details: This class will flow through the energies of Yang into Yin. The first half will be focused on dynamic vinyasa-style asana to connect with and work the physical body through strong postures, an awareness of breath, a deep connection to sensation and body-mindfulness. The second half is designed to stimulate the parasympathetic nervous system with long floor-based postures, supported by meditation and mindfulness techniques to calm, ground and reset.

sessions.title: LATIN-FUSION DANCE
sessions.details: A joyful, high-energy class blending Latin dance styles with contemporary movement and expressive flow. In this 60-minute session, you’ll explore rhythm, musicality, and full-body movement through accessible choreography and playful combinations. Open to all levels, the class offers options and variations so you can move at your own pace—whether you’re new to dance or looking to deepen your groove. Expect a supportive atmosphere, a good sweat, and plenty of space to connect with the music, your body, and the joy of movement. No partner needed. Come as you are, leave energised and inspired.

sessions.title: HANDSTANDS- POP-UP
sessions.details: Inversions are a fun and challenging way of building strength and challenging the mind. If you are starting your handstand journey but are struggling to kick up and catch the balance or are married to the wall, these are perfect classes for you. We will learn some wrist, shoulder and body tension conditioning drills and unpick how we kick up to a handstand. We will touch on how to safely fall out of a handstand and discuss how important tackling fear is. We will work in partners and small groups to support each other.

sessions.title: YANG TO YIN
sessions.details: This class will flow through the energies of Yang into Yin. The first half will be focused on dynamic vinyasa-style asana to connect with and work the physical body through strong postures, an awareness of breath, a deep connection to sensation and body-mindfulness. The second half is designed to stimulate the parasympathetic nervous system with long floor-based postures, supported by meditation and mindfulness techniques to calm, ground and reset.

sessions.title: ROCKET
sessions.details: Rocket is deeply rooted in Ashtanga. It is a series of standing, balancing and inverted postures all linked by vinyasas (movement flows), incorporating basic poses with challenging arm balances, inversions and back bends Inversions are lot of fun, but they also prove to be some of the most challenging postures. Some of the upside down postures are designed to energise us, others are designed to calm us down. No matter if you are a newbie or an old hat to inversions this class will be fun, will get you sweating and see the world from another perspective -upside down.

Suitable for most levels, this class is a beautiful way to care for your body, move more freely, and give yourself the reset you didn’t know you needed.

sessions.title: SELF LYMPHATIC DRAINAGE WORKSHOP
sessions.details: Your lymphatic system is one of the body’s most important yet often overlooked systems. Responsible for helping move waste, excess fluid and cellular debris, it plays a vital role in how we look, feel and function. In this workshop, you’ll learn a simple and effective self-lymphatic drainage routine designed to support the face, head, neck, abdomen and legs. Through gentle touch, breath and movement, you’ll discover how to encourage flow throughout the body and develop a deeper understanding of how your own system works. This is not about quick fixes or complicated protocols. It’s about learning practical skills that can become part of your regular self-care routine. Expect to leave feeling lighter, calmer, clearer and more connected to your body, with a routine you can return to whenever you need it. Many people choose to attend monthly or quarterly, using the workshop as a dedicated reset for both body and mind. Over time, the techniques become second nature, allowing you to confidently support your own lymphatic health at home. Simple. Empowering. Something you’ll use for life. HOW TO PREPARE Please arrive well hydrated and, if possible, drink a little water before the workshop. Wear comfortable clothing that allows you to move and easily access areas such as the collarbones, neck and abdomen. Leggings, joggers, shorts, a vest or a loose-fitting top are all perfect. We’ll be working with self-massage around the collarbones, face, neck and belly, so it’s helpful to avoid anything too tight or restrictive. Loose enough trousers that can be gently pulled down at the waist to access the lower abdomen are ideal. As we’ll be moving from a workout into the lymphatic workshop, comfort is key. Please also bring: * A small, clean face towel. * Clean hands and a clean face ready for the session. * Your own face and body oil (or cream) that you’re happy to apply to your skin, as we’ll be working on both the face and full body. No experience is needed — just bring yourself, an open mind and your own two hands.

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Benji Knapper
sessions.details: At the beginning of class we bring awareness to any tension in the body, moving through slower exercises we start to dance & find space to play with freedom in fluidity of movement. This then is applied to floor work, finding ways we can work efficiently, moving in and around the floor to start to build a safe speed in sequences and crossings to then increase the pace and physicality. We start to create heat in the body allowing us to play with activation and surrender to move bigger through exercises. Placing importance on finding 'form and shape' and then breaking it. The class works of the principles of pace, consideration and flow to find great physicality in phrases/form and the ability to cut/shift through space in virtuosic movement, drawing on elements of Cunningham, Release , ballet and floorwork

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Jemima Colins
sessions.details: Jemima's movement practice exists at the intersection of heightened listening, articulated expression and archival embodiment. The class is a combination of somatic mobilisations, improvisations and set materials; through which the accumulation of textures and embodiment can act as a tool to layer onto set material. Encouraging a communal collective exchange as well individual enquiry, through a class of high-physicality and awareness.

sessions.title: ProDance class with William James
sessions.details: JJ’s class will focus on tuning into the body to find joy, release and satisfaction from the inside out. Starting slow with grounding techniques to regulate the parasympathetic nervous system, we will click through the stages from calm to catharsis. We will aim to tune into the truth of what the body needs and let desire steer the flesh, gradually building to a peak of fun, enjoyment and abandon!

sessions.title: Couples Massage Workshop with RE-SET PAUSE
sessions.details: Massage is one of the most heartfelt ways to show care, a simple, meaningful way to connect through touch. Join us for a two-hour Couple’s Massage Workshop, designed for partners, friends, or family members who’d like to share a moment of relaxation and reconnection. You’ll learn gentle, soothing techniques, how to create a calming atmosphere, and ways to communicate through mindful touch for a deeply restorative experience. Fully clothed and performed on the floor, this practice is easy to recreate at home — with optional oils for extra comfort. The session is pregnancy-friendly and suitable for all levels. **Please note: this workshop is for personal enrichment and does not provide professional certification. One ticket per person is required.

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Alleyne Dance
sessions.details: This is a physical Contemporary Class, which will involve strong, complex and technical sequences in and out of the floor, focusing on quality, initiation, performance and stamina. Each session will begin with a different aspect of movement study, focusing on the articulation of hands, isolations, spirals, breath and flying. Improvisation will also be explored as part of self-development, stemming from a physical intention. The desired outcomes for the workshop are to develop the dancers' creative mind, driven from passion, determination, strength, musicality and most importantly an intention and honesty in whatever choice they make during the session.
